En: Rust peels off the bent iron posts of the swing. The wind whistles through the skeleton of the small house behind you. There's desolation... everywhere.
Zh: 锈迹从秋千弯曲的铁杆上剥落。风从你身后小屋的骨架间呼啸而过。一种悲哀的情绪……蔓延各处。

En: What happened here?
Zh: “这里发生了什么事？”

En: What happened here?
Zh: 这里发生了什么事？

En: In this yard? The lieutenant looks at the small building. A flock of grey swallows takes off in the distance.
Zh: “这个院子吗？”警督看着那栋小建筑。一群灰雁从远处振翅高飞。

En: Someone thought they could have a summer house in a *bloc obscur*. For cheap. It didn't work out. They abandoned it about a decade ago.
Zh: “有人觉得他们可以在*黑暗驻地*拥有一栋度假小屋。很便宜的那种。不过没有成功。大概10年前吧，他们就抛弃这里了。”

En: He's assessing the situation. How long ago was it abandoned.
Zh: 他在评估眼下的状况，它废弃的时间到底有多久。

En: Wait, what's a *bloc obscur?*
Zh: “等等，*黑暗驻地*是什么？”

En: So this part of the coast is a *bloc obscur*?
Zh: “所以说这片海岸是*黑暗驻地*？”

En: A black bloc, a part of the city left unrenovated after the war. Or one that has fallen to gang violence. Or has become inhospitable in some other way.
Zh: “黑暗驻地，是城市在战后没能得到修缮的部分，陷入帮派暴力的地区，或者是因为其他一些原因变得不适宜居住的地方。”

En: On aerial photos, bloc obscures look like dark squares. Hence their name.
Zh: 从航拍照片上看，黑暗驻地看起来就像是黑色的方块，所以因此得名。

En: Practically. It's not an official term in any way, but... he spreads his arms, "look around -- no sewage, broken power lines, crime, drunks... Life is tough in the blocs. It's no place to build a summer house."
Zh: “基本上是。虽然它并不是一个官方术语，不过……”他伸直胳膊，“看看周围——没有下水道，破损的电力线，犯罪，醉汉……在这些地区生活可不容易。这可不是造度假小屋的好地方。”

En: At least they left some old music behind. (Tap on the tape you picked up)
Zh: “至少他们留下了一些古老的音乐。”（拍拍你捡到的磁带。）

En: Maybe they left something useful behind?
Zh: “也许他们留下了一些有用的东西？”

En: In this yard? Someone thought they could have a summer house on the beach. For cheap. It didn't work out. Looks like they abandoned it.
Zh: 这个院子吗？有人觉得他们可以海滩边拥有一栋度假小屋。很便宜的那种。不过没有成功。看来他们已经抛弃这里了。

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维]

En: Yet someone used to live here. Laugh here. Thought it was a good idea to build a *swing* even...
Zh: 但是还有人曾经住在这里，在这里欢笑。我甚至觉得在这里造个*秋千*是个不错的主意……

En: Yes -- and you picked it up, because you're a post-apocalyptic scavenger who collects trash and magnesium blisters. He gives you a weary smile.
Zh: “是的——而且你把它捡了起来，因为你是个后启示录时代的拾荒者，专门收集垃圾和镁疱。”他露出一个疲倦的笑容。

En: Yes -- and you picked it up as part of the Jamrock shuffle. He gives you a weary smile.
Zh: “是的——而且你把当做加姆洛克开箱步的一部分捡起来了。”他朝你露出一个疲倦的笑容。

En: It's not meant as nagging, just an observation.
Zh: 这不是想唠叨什么，只是观察。

En: We should move. I don't think we will solve the murder with forays into the urban hinterland. At least in this phase of the investigation.
Zh: “我们该走了。深入城市腹地应该不能帮我们解决这起凶杀案。至少在调查的这个阶段不行。”

En: We should move. I don't think our suspect is in this particular yard. No place to hide, and besides, it's too close to Martinaise.
Zh: “我们该走了。我不觉得我们的嫌疑人就藏在这个院子里。不仅没地方藏身，而且离马丁内斯太近了。”

En: Yes -- for you to scavenge, like a post-apocalyptic scavenger who collects trash and errant magnesium blisters. He gives you a weary smile.
Zh: “是的——专门留给你的，就像一个后启示录时代的拾荒者，专门收集垃圾和镁疱。”他露出一个疲倦的笑容。

En: Yes -- for you to pick up as part of the Jamrock shuffle. He gives you a weary smile.
Zh: “是的——而且你把当做加姆洛克开箱步的一部分捡起来了。”他朝你露出一个疲倦的笑容。

En: You're right -- it's no place. Let's go. [Finish]
Zh: “你说的对——不是这个地方。我们走吧。”[结束]

En: A long time ago, someone used to live here. Laugh here. Thought it was a good idea to build a *swing* even... an intense loneliness comes over you.
Zh: 很久以前，有人曾经住在这里，在这里欢笑。我甚至觉得在这里搭一座*秋千*是个不错的主意……一种强烈的孤独感涌上你的心头。

En: This is a good place for a nice *sad* drink. Uncork a velvety bottle of red wine and think about life for a moment. Really *philosophise*.
Zh: 这里是喝*闷酒*的好地方。撬开一瓶美味的红酒，想一想自己的生活。真的很*哲学*。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: One of the flyers has gotten stuck to the branches of a small brush. The sound of it flapping in the wind is an echo of Van Eyck.
Zh: 一张海报卡在小灌木丛的枝丫之间。它在风中翻飞的声音就是凡·艾克的回响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Did that really happen just now?
Zh: 刚才真的发生了吗？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: [Ignore thought.]
Zh: [忽略思维。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: In the back of your head, you feel the rapid beat of anodic music... then it fades away.
Zh: 在你脑后，你感觉到阳极音乐的快速节拍……然后逐渐消散。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Your outstretched hand basks in the warm spring sun.
Zh: 你伸出双手去感受春日暖阳的温度。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Grab it quickly.
Zh: 快点抓住它。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: As your hand reaches for the small rectangle of glossy paper, it snaps free from the branch...
Zh: 当你的手碰到那张长方形的光面纸时，它从枝丫中偷偷溜走了……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: What the shit?
Zh: 什么鬼？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Cold drops of rain fall onto your outstretched hand. A slight tremble passes through it...
Zh: 金色的雨滴坠落在你伸出的手上。一阵轻微的颤动穿梭而过……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Flakes of snow fall onto your outstretched hand. A slight tremble passes through it...
Zh: 片片雪花飘落在你伸出的手上。一阵轻微的颤动穿梭而过……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Your outstretched hand casts a deep shadow on the ground...
Zh: 你伸出的双手在地面上投下一道深深的阴影……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Cold drops of rain fall onto your outstretched hand. A slight tremble passes through it...
Zh: 金色的雨滴坠落在你伸出的手上。一阵轻微的颤动穿梭而过……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Large flakes of snow fall onto your outstretched hand. A slight tremble passes through it...
Zh: 大片大片的雪花飘落在你伸出的手上。一阵轻微的颤动穿梭而过……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: ...and is swept away, out of existence.
Zh: ……然后被一扫而光，不复存在。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Am I hallucinating?
Zh: 我产生幻觉了吗？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Okay, I'm done with this. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 好吧，这个到此为止了。[完成思维。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: No.
Zh: 不。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: At the back of your head you feel the rapid beat of anodic music... then it fades away.
Zh: 在你脑后，你感觉到阳极音乐的快速节拍……然后逐渐消散。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Yes. It's likely triggered by a focal epileptic seizure in your temporal lobe.
Zh: 没错。可能是你颞叶的局灶性癫痫发作引起的。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Is it dangerous?
Zh: 很危险吗？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Oh my god, I'm going to die!
Zh: 我的天啦，我要死了！

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: I don't know... probably. I'm not a doctor.
Zh: 我不知道……也许吧。我不是医生。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Yeah, probably, one day. But not this very minute.
Zh: 是啊，也许，有一天。但是这一分钟不会。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: And so all our lives become but a faded memory, an ephemeral vision of a Van Eyck concert flyer... blink or you'll miss it.
Zh: 你所有的生活都变成了褪色的记忆，如凡·艾克音乐会的传单一般短命……眨眼之间，就会消失不见。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: The lieutenant is looking at the spot you were reaching for. "Did you notice something important?"
Zh: 警督看向你伸手的方向。“你是注意到什么重要的东西了？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Kim, I think I'm seeing Van Eyck posters everywhere now.
Zh: “金，我想我现在到处都能看到凡·艾克的海报。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: No... I don't think so. It was just a reflection or something. Never mind.
Zh: “不……我不这么觉得。这是个倒影或者什么的。没关系的。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: A GLIMPSE INTO THE PAST
Zh: 回顾过去

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: IT HAPPENED ONCE; LONG AGO
Zh: 它曾经发生过一次；很久以前

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Okay. Well. I'm not going to ask who Van Eyck is. I'm just going to assume it's your business and move on, okay? He raises his collar.
Zh: “好吧。我不会去问这个凡·艾克是谁。我会假定这是你的事，然后我们继续前进，好吗？”他竖起了衣领。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: He nods. "Let's get going. We have a case to solve."
Zh: 他点点头。“我们走吧，还有案子要破呢。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Never mind. Let's get going. [Finish thought.]
Zh: “没事了。我们走吧。”[完成思维。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: The shadows play tricks on your mind...
Zh: 阴影在玩弄你的大脑……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / arno van eyck after
En: Things in the night are not what they seem to be...
Zh: 夜晚的东西感觉都变得不像它们自己了……

En: Can *I* try to get in though?
Zh: 不过*我*能试着闯进去吗？

En: Not this time. The opposition is insurmountable. But I like the spirit -- have some *points*! It's lonely and cold without points.
Zh: 这次不行。对方很难对付。不过我喜欢这种精神——应该得点*分数*。若是没有的话，那就太孤独、太清冷了。

En: Kim, you think she's in there? (Point at the boarded up building.)
Zh: “金，你觉得她在里面吗？”（指着被封锁的建筑。）

En: The suspect? God, I hope not -- I can't see a way in... He inspects the bars. "Though many have tried."
Zh: “嫌疑人吗？老天，我希望没有——我没看见什么能进去的地方……”他检查了一下木板。“不过倒是有很多人试过。”

En: And dangerous. Dangerous too.
Zh: 而且很危险，非常危险。

En: There's no way the perp is in here, officer. Look how scarred the boards are -- all attempts to pry them off have failed.
Zh: 那个罪犯不可能躲在这里，警官。看看这些伤痕累累的木板——所有想把它们撬开的企图都失败了。

En: Nothing more to do here then. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 这里没什么可以做的了。[完成思维。]

En: The light vanishes inside the concrete slit. The structure goes deep under the earth.
Zh: 光线消失在混凝土的裂隙中。这个结构是深入地底深处的。

En: [Finish thought.]
Zh: [完成思维。]

En: What's in there?
Zh: 里面是什么？

En: Maybe it's just a storm drain for the sewer?
Zh: 也许只是下水道的雨水沟？

En: Kim, any idea what's down there?
Zh: “金，知道下面是什么吗？”

En: No idea. He takes his glasses off to polish. "Could be connected to one of the buildings around here..."
Zh: “不知道。”他取下眼镜擦拭着。“也许跟这附近的某栋建筑是相连的吧……”

En: Think we might find Ruby down there?
Zh: “你觉得我们能不能在下面找到露比？”

En: We might find her down *somewhere* -- there's an old storm drain system beneath Martinaise that's mostly collapsed. Revachol's sewage system has been built and rebuilt four or five times now.
Zh: “我们也许是能在下面的*某个地方*找到她——马丁内斯下面有一个老旧的暴雨排水系统，不过基本上已经塌陷了。瑞瓦肖的下水道系统到现在已经来来回回翻修4、5次了。”

En: Yell "Ho!" into the slit.
Zh: 往裂缝地喊一声：“哟吼！”

En: There's no echo -- and no answer.
Zh: 没有回音——也无人应答。

En: The silence of god? Sorry...
Zh: 神的沉默？抱歉……

En: He puts his glasses back on. "In conclusion -- she could be under any building."
Zh: 他重新戴上眼镜。“总而言之——她可能藏在任意一栋建筑下面。”

En: But not in there?
Zh: “但是这里没有吗？”

En: He looks into the slit and sighs. "I hope not."
Zh: 他看了一眼裂缝，叹了口气。“我希望没有。”

En: [Finish thought.]
Zh: [完成思维。]

En: I had a partner once. They called him Eyes, because he had to show me things. It's that bad.
Zh: “我以前有个搭档。大家叫他眼睛，因为他必须指东西给我看。就有那么糟糕。”

En: There's rust and corrosion on the bars, they're foaming with it. And a small layer of white salt from the sea.
Zh: 铁条上有锈迹和腐蚀的痕迹，起泡。上面还有一层薄薄的白色海盐。

En: Can you still shoot, though?
Zh: “即便这样，你还能开枪？”

En: Just nod. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 只是点点头。[完成思维。]

En: This partner of his, Eyes -- things didn't end well. It saddens him to say his name. Don't even ask. He wouldn't answer. Maybe some other small talk?
Zh: 他的搭档，眼睛——结局并不是很好。说出他的名字都会让他感到伤心。别问了。他不会回答的。也许可以聊点别的？

En: Well enough, actually. It's odd how that works. I'm no sharpshooter, but I pass my shooting courses 7 out of 10.
Zh: “其实吧，还可以。那样居然能行，挺奇怪的。我不是神枪手，不过10分的射击课程，我7分通过了。”

En: His hands lead the way when it comes to that. And his shoulders. It's reflex.
Zh: 说到这一点，他会用手做展示。还有他的肩膀。这是一种本能反应。

En: Lieutenant, can you make out what's inside? (Point at the windows.)
Zh: “警督，你能看出里面有什么吗？”（指向窗户。）

En: No. He shakes his head. The windows rattle in their frames. "I won't even try. You know..." He takes his glasses off.
Zh: “不能。”他摇摇头。窗户在窗框中咯吱作响。“我连试都不会试。你知道的……”他取下眼镜。

En: The remaining windows rattle from a strong gust of wind. They're covered in a thick layer of grime. They must have been like this for forty years.
Zh: 仅存的窗户被一阵狂风吹地咔哒直响，上面盖着一层厚厚的尘垢。这40年来，它们肯定一直都是这样。

En: Try to see inside.
Zh: 试着往里看。

En: Dripping water falls from a high place. All you can see is the shadow of a collapsing staircase.
Zh: 高处有水在滴落。你唯一能看到的，只有一座坍塌楼梯的阴影。

En: Somehow he doesn't want to dwell on it...
Zh: 不知道为什么，他不想细说……

En: This is it -- the scene of the party. The fire pit, cigarettes, and empty bottles all evidence it.
Zh: 就是这里了——派对现场。火堆、香烟、还有空瓶子，全都是证据。

En: Not as such. I'm talking about what came after -- the party scene.
Zh: 不太对。我说的是那之后的事了——派对现场。

En: Looks like they were here a while, judging from all the bottles. The sunken motor carriage provided them a focal point -- like a goose ice-sculpture or a chocolate fountain.
Zh: 从那些瓶子来看，他们在这里待过一段时间。那辆沉没的汽車给他们提供了一个焦点——就像一座鸿雁冰雕，或者是巧克力喷泉。

En: Hold up -- don't you mean scene of the crime?
Zh: 等等——你说的不是犯罪现场吗？

En: Yeah. Sure does look like a lot of folks partied here.
Zh: 没错。看起来确实有很多人在这里开过派对。

En: (Dismiss thought.)
Zh: （摒弃这个想法。）

En: Hey, Kim. Looks like we've had a couple of party-goers here.
Zh: “喂，金。看来有一群派对动物在这里待过。”

En: Guess I'll be on my way. (Finish thought.)
Zh: 看来我得走了。（完成思维。）

En: Looks like it.
Zh: “好像是这样没错。”

En: Hey -- let's keep moving, detective. The lieutenant adjusts his glasses.
Zh: 警督调整了一下眼镜，点点头。“我说——我们还是继续前进吧，警探。”

En: Not as such, no. Let's keep moving, detective.
Zh: 警督摇摇头。“不太像。我们还是继续前进吧，警探。”

En: Looks like they had a great time laughing here.
Zh: “看来他们在这里笑得很开心。”

En: This was some kind of theatre to them. A circus production by a great clown.
Zh: “对于他们来说是某种剧院。一个伟大小丑的马戏团表演。”

En: Was this party against the law? On the ice like this -- it was probably a public danger.
Zh: “这种派对是违法的吗？在这种冰面上——可能会对公众造成危险。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Great news -- the boat is big enough for a grown man like you to fit underneath it in a supine position.
Zh: 好消息——这艘船正好够一个像你这样的成年人仰卧在里面。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Wait, what would I be doing under there?
Zh: 等等，我躺下能干什么？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Great news, I found somewhere new to sleep.
Zh: “好消息，我找到一个新的可以睡觉的地方了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: I don't know? Sleeping? What do people do under boats? This is merely a measurement from your visual cortex, do with it what you want.
Zh: 我不知道？睡觉？大家都在船下面干嘛？这只是你视觉皮质的一个测量结果，你想怎么做就怎么做吧。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Oh no. He's pretty much a bum now.
Zh: 哦不。现在他几乎就是个真正的流浪汉了。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Huh?

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: I said: great news, I found somewhere to sleep. (Point at the boat). "Under this boat here. It'll be free."
Zh: “我说：好消息，我找到可以睡觉的地方了。”（指着那艘船）“就在船下面，而且是免费的。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: I can pretty much finish the case from under that boat there. It's dry, weatherproof, and *free of charge*.
Zh: “我几乎可以在那艘船下面处理完这个案子。那里很干燥，防水，而且*不要钱*。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: I'm gonna live under a boat now.
Zh: “现在我要住在船底下了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Sarcastic self pity is not what we need at this moment, officer.
Zh: “我们现在不需要这种尖酸刻薄的自哀自怜，警官。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Self pity is not what we need at this moment, officer.
Zh: “我们现在不需要你的自哀自怜，警官。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: I understand the situation looks grim, but we must continue with our investigation.
Zh: “我明白，现在的情况确实看起来有些糟糕，但我们必须继续调查。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Sleeping. Was it not decided that you're a *hobocop* -- more hobo than cop -- or was that fine irony that your visual cortex somehow misunderstood?
Zh: 睡觉。难道不是这样决定的吗，你是个*流浪警探*——比起警察更像是个流浪汉——或者是你的视觉皮质不知怎么的误解了这个绝妙的讽刺？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: The elements are rough around here. I don't know about this...
Zh: 周围的环境实在是太艰苦了。我不太确定……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Think of it as a salvation.
Zh: 就把这当成是一种救赎。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: You have a home, somewhere. All cops do. When this is done you can return.
Zh: 你的家就在某个地方。所有警察都一样。当这一切结束，你就可以回到那个地方。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Great news then. I do need somewhere cosy.
Zh: 这是个好消息。我确实需要一个舒适的地方。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: No, wait -- sorry. My bad. There's a rotting dog carcass under there and old fish too. You wouldn't fit next to the mummified canine's mausoleum.
Zh: 不对，等等——对不起。我的错。那下面有一具腐烂的野狗尸体，还有鱼。犬科木乃伊的坟墓可不适合你。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Damn dog, taking up all your space. The fish smell is too rank too, though.
Zh: 该死的狗，占用了你全部的空间。不过，鱼的味道也够销魂的。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: You have a home, somewhere. All cops do. When this is done you can return.
Zh: 你的家就是某个地方。所有警察都一样。当这一切结束，你就可以回到那个地方。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / living under the boat
En: Just finish the case and you'll probably have somewhere to go. Hopefully.
Zh: 只要解决这个案子，也许你也有个地方可以归去。希望如此。

En: It's only a red chair. Just a red chair in an empty shack -- with what looked like a dusty bow tie on the shelf. Nothing to see here, right?
Zh: 只是一把红椅子。一个空旷棚屋里的红椅子——屋里的架子上像是放着一条灰尘扑扑的领带。

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: Sounds like we need to investigate it, then.
Zh: 听起来我们需要调查一下。

En: In someone's abandoned shack. On the coast. In Martinaise... He looks at you. "In Revachol."
Zh: “在某人废弃的棚屋里。海岸边。马丁内斯……”他看着你。“瑞瓦肖。”

En: Hey Kim, where are we?
Zh: “喂，金，我们这是在哪？”

En: Investigate the blood red chair? You're going to leave it alone, Harry. That's what you're going to do.
Zh: 调查这把血红色的椅子？你还是放着它别管了，哈里。你必须这么做。

En: He's afraid you've forgotten suddenly.
Zh: 他害怕你会突然忘记。

En: You suddenly feel afraid of the chair. It looks like blood is peeling off it...
Zh: 你突然有点害怕这张椅子。看起来像有鲜血从上面滑落……

En: What's with the red chair?
Zh: “这把红椅子是怎么回事？”

En: What's with the red bow tie? (Show it to him.)
Zh: “这条红领带是怎么回事？”（拿给他看。）

En: It's just a piece of clothing you've picked up. You do that, officer -- you collect clothes sometimes.
Zh: “只是你捡到的一件衣物。你是会这么做，警官——有时候你会收集衣服。”

En: He sounds a little worried. It's an odd habit.
Zh: 他听起来有些担心。这是个奇怪的习惯。

En: Don't be worried, lieutenant. Clothes are therapeutic.
Zh: 别担心，警督。衣服是有益健康的。

En: You better not fucking think about ditching me for some shitty cum stained bow tie. You and I are gearing up for the *final showdown* that's gonna be off *all walls*. You wait and see.
Zh: 你他妈最好别想着因为那条劣质又肮脏的领带甩开我。你和我正在为*最终决战*做准备，而且一定会*盛况空前*。你等着看好吧。

En: You took *me* off for that shit? YOU FUCKING IDIOT! Put the horrific neck tie back on immediately -- or you will miss out on the FINAL SHOWDOWN. This is your last warning. Put me back on.
Zh: 你就因为那坨屎把*我*取下来？你他妈是白痴吧！马上给我把那条可怕的领带戴回去——不然你会错过最终决战的。最后警告你一次。把我戴回去。

En: Your neck tie sounds very scared. It's unsafe not to wear it. Put it back.
Zh: 你的领带听起来很吓人。不戴好像不太安全，还是戴回去吧。

En: Yes. Agreed. You should never take your neck tie off. Ever.
Zh: 没错。我同意。你不应该把领带取下来，永远也不要。

En: Your neck tie sounds very scared. NEVER take it off.
Zh: 你的领带听起来很吓人。永远别把它取下来。

En: Nothing. It's just a piece of furniture. He looks at the chair gathering dust.
Zh: “没什么。只是一件家具。”他看着椅子上堆积的灰尘。

En: Red paint is peeling off it. Looks like blood, no?
Zh: 红色的油漆从上面剥落。看起来像鲜血，不是吗？

En: Could our suspect be staying here?
Zh: “我们的嫌疑人会不会待在这里？”

En: Here? He looks around. "I don't see signs of recent habitation. This place is abandoned."
Zh: “这里？”他看了看周围。“我没看见最近有人居住过的痕迹。这里已经废弃了。”

En: See? He doesn't like the *vibration* from the red chair either.
Zh: 看见没？他也不喜欢这把红椅子的*波动*。

En: All right. Let's move. [Finish thought.]
Zh: “好吧。我们走。”[完成思维。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: You set the apparatus back on the ground, among the reeds.
Zh: 你把装置放回地面，隐藏在芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Just dead and dying locusts, and the slow swaying of surrounding reeds.
Zh: 只有死去的和奄奄一息的蝗虫，还有周围缓缓摇曳的芦苇。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the empty trap. That one was by the canal.
Zh: 蝗虫的状况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这个陷阱不是空的。那个在运河边。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the empty trap. That one Morell was setting up, near the cryptozoologists camp.
Zh: 蝗虫的状况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这个陷阱不是空的。那个是莫雷尔设置的，就在神秘动物学家的营地旁边。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the empty trap. That one was in Land's End, in the northern tip of this peninsula.
Zh: 蝗虫的状况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这个陷阱不是空的。那个在地之角，这座半岛的最北端。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the *empty* trap. That must have been another one.
Zh: 蝗虫的情况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这不是*空*陷阱。肯定还有其他的。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: All the traps are stocked now -- and still no sight of the phasmid. Return to Morell and let him know.
Zh: 现在所有陷阱都设置好了——但还是没有看见竹节虫。回到莫雷尔那里，把消息告诉他。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: He purses his lips. "Perhaps our cryptozoologists have competition in the form of an actual entomologist? Or someone else is sabotaging them? I could present more theories, but then I would be taking this on as a case -- which I'm *not*."
Zh: 他咬起嘴唇。“也许我们的神秘动物学家与真正的昆虫学家之间存在竞争关系？又或者其他什么人在蓄意破坏？我可以提出更多的理论，不过那样的话，可能就要立案了——但我并*不想*这么做。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Locusts are crawling around in the trap, confused but uneaten. You see no carnivorous *reed-phasmid* gorging on them.
Zh: 蝗虫在陷阱里爬行，没有被吃掉，但却十分迷惑。你没看见食肉的*芦苇-竹节虫*在狼吞虎咽地享用它们。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: There's nothing else to do with the traps at this time. They're all just lying around, gathering dust and rainwater.
Zh: 现在已经没什么可以做的了。陷阱只是躺在那里，收集灰尘和雨水。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Your voice echoes on the coast, carried by a gust of cold wind.
Zh: 一阵冷风卷起你的声音，在海岸边发出回响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: You've checked all the traps now. There's nothing else to do with them. The cryptozoologists await your report.
Zh: 现在所有陷阱已经检查完毕。没什么需要做的了。神秘动物学家还在等待你的报告。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Surely, he repeats and looks at the sea, then at you putting the trap back on the ground. "Anyway, the air is nice and fresh here..."
Zh: “当然了，”他望着大海又重复了一遍，然后看着你把陷阱放回地面上。“不管怎么说，这里的空气不错，很新鲜……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: A cold gust of air dries your sweaty face and you look to the dark shadow the Feld building in the distance, drawing you to it. What a strange sensation... Once this is done, should you ask the wind again?
Zh: 一阵冷风吹干你脸上的汗水，你看着远处菲尔德大厦的黑色阴影，慢慢被它吸引。多么奇怪的一种感觉……等这些结束之后，你再问问风？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Let's keep going. The next one is the *lucky* one.
Zh: “我们走吧。下一个肯定就*走运*了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: It'll be in the next one. *Surely*.
Zh: “肯定在下一个陷阱里面。*绝对的*。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The lieutenant stretches his back.
Zh: 警督挺直后背。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: I'm beginning to feel... somewhat sorry for the little guys.
Zh: “我开始有点……为这些小家伙感到抱歉。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Poor things...
Zh: 可怜的家伙……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Big surprise. The lieutenant grins mirthlessly. "Anyway -- one down, three to go."
Zh: “大惊喜。”警督阴沉地咧嘴笑了。“不管怎么说——排除一个，还剩三个。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: I meant no offence, just... The lieutenant doesn't know how to finish the sentence. He looks at you, putting the trap back on the ground.
Zh: “我不想冒犯，只是……”警督不知道该如何说完这句话。他看着你把陷阱放回地面。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: No need to grin. I'm not *expecting* to find anything. I'm helping some citizens and getting some fresh air.
Zh: “不用笑的这么勉强。我没*期望*能找到什么。我只是在帮助市民，然后顺便呼吸一下新鲜空气。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The locusts, dazed from being transported, slowly begin to acclimate to their new surroundings.
Zh: 刚经历了长途跋涉的蝗虫还有些头晕，不过也开始慢慢地适应起周围的新环境来。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Release the locusts into the empty trap.
Zh: 把蝗虫放进空陷阱。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: They're not really going to get the chance to get comfortable here.
Zh: 它们舒舒服服地生活在这里的机会基本为零。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: We did *sort of* promise to tell them, didn't we... He seems to regret the fact.
Zh: “我们确实*好像*答应过要告诉他们，是吧……”他好像为这个事实感到有些后悔。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: I do get the feeling that someone or something may have messed with the trap...
Zh: “我确实感觉到好像有什么人，或者什么东西在破坏陷阱……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The wind picks up here, near the cape's end, surrounding the narrow strip of land from three cardinal directions. It's cold for this time of year.
Zh: 海角边缘附近，大风从三个方向包围住这片狭长的土地。每年的这个时候，总是特别的冷。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds shake sadly in the coastal breeze, weighed down by rain. They seem to be waiting for something.
Zh: 芦苇在海岸的微风中悲伤地颤抖着，被雨滴打得直不起腰来。它们像是在等待着什么。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds shake sadly in the coastal breeze. Snow specks the stalks. Most of it melts quickly, relinquishing form to darkness.
Zh: 芦苇在海岸的微风中悲伤地颤抖着。斑驳的雪花点缀着茎杆，大部分很快融化不见，隐身于黑暗之中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds shake sadly in the coastal breeze. Snow specks the stalks. Most of it melts quickly. The reeds seem to be waiting for something.
Zh: 芦苇在海岸的微风中悲伤地颤抖着。斑驳的雪花点缀着茎杆，大部分很快融化不见。芦苇好像在等待着什么。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds shake sadly in the coastal breeze. They seem to be waiting for something -- some secret concealed in the night's warmthless robe.
Zh: 芦苇在海岸的微风中悲伤地颤抖，好像在等待着——隐藏在夜晚那冰冷长袍下的秘密。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds sway in the coastal breeze. They seem to be waiting for something.
Zh: 芦苇在海岸的微风下轻轻摇摆，像是在等待着什么。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Right. It does not look like he thinks you're right. "Anyway, that's for the cryptozoologists to figure out now." He adds, for clarity: "*We're* not cryptozoologists -- we're cops."
Zh: “没错。”但他看起来并不觉得你是对的。“不管怎么说，现在这是神秘动物学家的需要解决的问题了。”为了澄清，他又补充了一句：“*我们*不是神秘动物学家——我们是警察。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Okay. But after that we get back to our own assignments. There really is *no* explanation for this detour at this point of the investigation.
Zh: “好吧。但是在那之后，我们要继续自己的任务。调查进行到这个阶段，像这样绕道而行真的找不到*任何*合理的解释。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Okay. But after that we get back to our own assignments. We've done a thorough survey of the coast now -- just running around is no longer useful.
Zh: “好吧。但是在那之后，我们要继续自己的任务。既然现在已经对海岸进行了十分彻底的搜查——再这样到处跑来跑去就没什么用了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: No you weren't. He looks at the sea, then you -- laying the trap back down on the ground.
Zh: “不，你没有。”他看着大海，然后看着你——把陷阱放回地面。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Yeah, it probably *wasn't* the phasmid, but still. Morell needs to know.
Zh: “是啊，它也许*不是*竹节虫，不过莫雷尔还是需要知道这一点。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Damn, I was hoping it would be in the *first* one.
Zh: “该死，我还希望这会是*第一*个呢。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Nothing has changed about this trap. You need to check the others.
Zh: 这个陷阱没有任何变化。你需要检查一下其它的。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Pause and just stand there.
Zh: 停下来，只是静静站着。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧。”[离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds whisper around the sepulchral trap, as though suddenly filled with fear. Even the snow does not quiet them.
Zh: 芦苇围绕在阴森森的陷阱旁窃窃私语，好像突然被恐惧包围一样。就连雪花也无法给它们带去安慰。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds shudder in the falling rain, whispering. It is as though the sepulchral trap has filled them with some strange, novel fear.
Zh: 芦苇在落雨中颤抖着，低语着。仿佛这个阴森森的陷阱让它们产生了某种奇怪而诡异的恐惧。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds whisper around the sepulchral trap, as though suddenly filled with fear.
Zh: 芦苇围绕在阴森森的陷阱旁窃窃私语，好像突然被恐惧包围一样。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Maybe they will still attract something?
Zh: “也许它们还能吸引一些东西？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Yes. Like a cat, or another teenage psychopath.
Zh: “是的。比如说一只猫，或者是又一个精神变态的青年。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Good, good. We should be courteous and tell the cryptozoologists that one of their traps was empty, but then we can get back to *our* assignment here.
Zh: “很好，很好。我们应该礼貌一点，告诉神秘动物学家有一个陷阱空了，然后继续回去完成*我们自己*的任务。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: But what if it *was* the phasmid? What if it ate them and got out?
Zh: “但是如果*真是*竹节虫呢？如果它把蝗虫全吃掉，然后跑出来了呢？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: You're right. But I still need to tell the cryptozoologists about this.
Zh: “你说得对。但我还是要跟神秘动物学家说一声。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Good. Now that's done. When can we get to our impending apocalypse of a murder investigation?
Zh: “很好。现在这个事情已经告一段落。我们什么时候才能看到凶杀案调查的启示呢？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: You're right. This crypto-research has been stupid.
Zh: “你说得对。这次的神秘动物学研究实在是太愚蠢了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: He stops you. "Don't answer that. It was a rhetorical question."
Zh: 他拦住了你。“别回答。这是个反问句。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Neither does the lieutenant. He just raises his collar -- it's cold out, as always.
Zh: 警督也没有言语。他只是竖起衣领——毕竟外面很冷，一如往常。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Look around.
Zh: 看看周围。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The lieutenant's gaze follows yours and lingers on the reeds, the sand, the water beyond. Then he turns back to you expectantly.
Zh: 警督追随着你的目光，先是停留在芦苇上，然后是沙子，最后是水面。接着，他满是期待地把目光转回到你身上。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds continue to hiss around you as you put the empty trap back on the ground. Time to consult the professionals -- Morell and Lena.
Zh: 芦苇不断地在你周围嘶嘶作响，你把空陷阱又放回到地面上。是时候去找专业人士咨询一下了——莫雷尔和莉娜。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: He doesn't want to, but if there is one more cryptozoological run-around, he *must* force the investigation back on track. This better be it...
Zh: 他不想这么做，但是如果再来一次神秘动物学的借口，他*肯定*会强迫调查回归正轨。最好就到此为止了……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Nothing but locusts in this trap as well. Definitely no cryptozoological monstrosity.
Zh: 这个陷阱也一样，除了蝗虫什么也没有。肯定没有神秘动物学意义上的畸形。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The trap feels light -- and silent -- as you pick it up. Something is different here...
Zh: 这个陷阱感觉很轻——还很安静。这里好像有点不太一样……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Look closer.
Zh: 再仔细看看。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: It takes you a moment, but finally you spot the last of Morell's traps. This one's partially obscured by the reeds...
Zh: 你花了一会儿功夫，总算找到了莫雷尔的最后一个陷阱。有一部分被芦苇给挡住了……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Man, it's lonely doing this alone. The wind seems to howl in a strange manner...
Zh: 伙计，一个人做这个也太孤独了。风似乎都在以一种奇怪的方式呼啸……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: No locusts!
Zh: 没有蝗虫！

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: This trap's not too hard to spot -- once you know what to look for. Keeping it hidden has not been a priority for the cryptozoologist.
Zh: 要找到这个陷阱并不太难——只要你明白自己在找什么。对于神秘动物学家来说，把它隐藏起来可不是最高优先级。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Yup. The next one has a *crab* trapped in it. He raises his collar -- it's windy. "These are just crab traps, you do realize?"
Zh: “是的。下一个里面困了只*螃蟹*。”他竖起衣领——起风了。“你有没有发现，这些只不过是抓螃蟹的陷阱？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Reach for the trap.
Zh: 伸手去碰陷阱。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: A familiar apparatus lies among the reeds -- another one of Morell's traps, weighed down by stones to keep it in place.
Zh: 一个熟悉的装置躺在芦苇丛中……又是莫雷尔的陷阱，上面压着石头，方便固定它的位置。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Again -- I want to make it absolutely *clear* that I don't really believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “再说一次——我要明确*声明*一点，我其实并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: I want to make it clear that I don't *actually* believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “我想声明一点，我*其实*并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: No phasmid either -- but still...
Zh: 也没有竹节虫——不过……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The netting is a little untidy, messier than the others. Like someone or something picked up the trap and shook it before dropping it back down on the ground.
Zh: 相比其他陷阱，这个的网更凌乱一些。就好像有人或者什么东西把陷阱捡了起来，摇了摇，然后又丢回地面。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Empty as all of them... he pants. "*One* more of these and we're done..." His face is red from the cold sea air. He crouches to catch his breath.
Zh: “跟其他的一样空……”他喘着气。“最多再看*一个*，我们就不做了……”他的脸被冰冷的海风吹得通红，蹲伏下身体喘着气。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: One more time, I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*再强调一遍，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*强调一下，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Nothing. No traces of the locusts *or* the Insulindian phasmid. What happened?
Zh: 什么也没有。没有蝗虫*或者*是伊苏林迪竹节虫的痕迹。出什么事了？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The lieutenant studies the trap with you. "Well, the bait worked on *something*. This doesn't mean it was a reed-monster, though. Unless you see one in there? I just see an empty trap..."
Zh: 警督跟你一起研究陷阱。“呃，诱饵对*某种东西*起作用了。但也并不意味着就是芦苇怪兽做的。除非你亲眼看见里面有？我只看到一个空陷阱……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Bummer it wasn't in here...
Zh: “可惜，它不在这里……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: (Immediately yell.) "It's empty!"
Zh: （立马大叫出来。）“它是空的！”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Look closer still.
Zh: 靠近查看。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Always up for a good jog -- otherwise, would I still be *on* this case with *you*? He smiles and raises his collar. It's windy.
Zh: “我一直好好坚持早起慢跑——不然的话，我还会跟*你*一起破*这个*案子吗？”他笑着竖起了衣领，突然起风了。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: You getting tired?
Zh: “你累了吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: I'm just... he breathes out. "Glad we haven't found some poor cat trapped in one of these."
Zh: “我只是……”他吐出一口气。“很高兴没在里面找到被困住的可怜小猫。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: No-no. I'm fine. I didn't mean to complain, it's just... He's short-winded. The sentence ends there.
Zh: “不。我很好。我不是想抱怨，只不过……”他有些气喘。话语到这里就结束了。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: How are you enjoying the cardio, lieutenant? I'm quite enjoying it myself.
Zh: “你喜欢有氧运动吗，警督？我自己觉得挺舒服的。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: Another empty trap, the lieutenant takes a note -- more out of habit than duty.
Zh: “又是个空陷阱，”警督记下笔记——相比职责，更像是出于一种习惯。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: There's a trap in the reeds at your feet. Looks like the same one you saw Morell set before -- same mesh, same wiring.
Zh: 你脚下的芦苇丛里有一个陷阱。跟之前看见的莫雷尔的陷阱一模一样——同样的网，同样的线。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: This trap is also full of panicked locusts. No sign of any cryptozoological beast inside.
Zh: 这个陷阱里面也满是惊慌失措的蝗虫，没有任何神秘动物学范畴的野兽存在。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds dance slowly around the empty trap. The trap's netting trembles, shaking off the snow. No insect sounds or movement anywhere.
Zh: 芦苇在空陷阱周围缓缓舞动。陷阱的网颤抖着，想把身上的雪抖落。到处都没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds dance slowly around the empty trap. The trap's netting trembles in the cold rain. No insect sounds or movement anywhere.
Zh: 芦苇在空陷阱周围缓缓舞动。陷阱的网在冰冷的雨滴中颤抖着。到处都没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds at lands end
En: The reeds dance slowly around the empty trap. The trap's netting trembles. No insect sounds or movement anywhere.
Zh: 芦苇在空陷阱周围缓缓舞动。陷阱的网在颤抖着。到处都没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The lieutenant's gaze follows yours and lingers on the reeds, the sand, the water beyond. Then he turns back to you expectantly.
Zh: 警督追随着你的目光，先是停留在芦苇上，然后是沙子，最后是水面。接着，他满是期待地把目光转回到你身上。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Reach for the trap.
Zh: 伸手去碰陷阱。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The lieutenant studies the trap with you. "Well, the bait worked on *something*. This doesn't mean it was a reed-monster, though. Unless you see one in there? I just see an empty trap..."
Zh: 警督跟你一起研究陷阱。“呃，诱饵对*某种东西*起作用了。但也并不意味着它就是芦苇怪兽。除非你亲眼看见里面有？我只看到一个空陷阱……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Look around.
Zh: 看看周围。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: You set the apparatus back on the ground, among the reeds.
Zh: 你把装置放回地面，隐藏在芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Your voice echoes on the coast, carried by a gust of cold wind.
Zh: 一阵冷风卷起你的声音，在海岸边发出回响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: This is the last of the traps, the one Morell just set. Checking it over -- he said -- is just a technicality.
Zh: 这是最后一个陷阱，莫雷尔刚刚设下的。他说——检查——只是个技术问题。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: This is the trap Morell just set. Checking it over, he said, is just a *technicality*, but...
Zh: 这是莫雷尔刚刚设下的陷阱。他说检查陷阱，只是一个*技术问题*，不过……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: No need to grin. I'm not *expecting* to find anything. I'm helping some citizens and getting some fresh air.
Zh: “不用笑的这么勉强。我没*期望*能找到什么。我只是在帮助市民，然后顺便呼吸一下新鲜空气。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: A cold gust of air dries your sweaty face and you look to the dark shadow the Feld building in the distance, drawing you to it. What a strange sensation... Once this is done, should you try to ask again?
Zh: 一阵冷风吹干你脸上的汗水，你看着远处菲尔德大厦的黑色阴影，慢慢被它吸引。真是种奇怪的感觉……等这些事做完之后，你是不是应该再试着问一次？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: No-no. I'm fine. I didn't mean to complain, it's just... He's short-winded. The sentence ends there.
Zh: “不。我很好。我不是想抱怨，只不过……”他有些气喘。话语到这里就结束了。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Nothing has changed about this trap. You need to check the others.
Zh: 这个陷阱没有任何变化。你需要检查一下其它的。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: There's nothing else to do with the traps at this time. They're all just lying around, gathering dust and rainwater.
Zh: 现在已经没什么可以做的了。陷阱只是躺在那里，收集灰尘和雨水。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Maybe they will still attract something?
Zh: “也许它们还能吸引一些东西？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: You've checked all the traps now. There's nothing else to do with them. The cryptozoologists await your report.
Zh: 现在所有陷阱已经检查完毕。没什么需要做的了。神秘动物学家还在等待你的报告。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The trap feels light -- and silent -- as you pick it up. Something is different here...
Zh: 这个陷阱感觉很轻——还很安静。这里好像有点不太一样……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: He doesn't want to, but if there is one more cryptozoological run-around, he *must* force the investigation back on track. This better be it...
Zh: 他不想这么做，但是如果再来一次神秘动物学的借口，他*肯定*会强迫调查回归正轨。最好就到此为止了……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: This trap is also full of panicked locusts. No sign of any cryptozoological beast inside.
Zh: 这个陷阱里面也满是惊慌失措的蝗虫，没有任何神秘动物学范畴的野兽存在。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Nothing but locusts in this trap as well. Definitely no cryptozoological monstrosity.
Zh: 这个陷阱也一样，除了蝗虫什么也没有。肯定没有神秘动物学意义上的畸形。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: We did *sort of* promise to tell them, didn't we... He seems to regret the fact.
Zh: “我们确实*好像*答应过要告诉他们，是吧……”他好像为这个事实感到有些后悔。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Right. It does not look like he thinks you're right. "Anyway, that's for the cryptozoologists to figure out now." He adds, for clarity: "*We're* not cryptozoologists -- we're cops."
Zh: “没错。”但他看起来并不觉得你是对的。“不管怎么说，现在这是神秘动物学家的需要解决的问题了。”为了澄清，他又补充了一句：“*我们*不是神秘动物学家——我们是警察。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: No locusts!
Zh: 没有蝗虫！

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Look closer.
Zh: 再仔细看看。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Okay. But after that we get back to our own assignments. There is no justification for this detour at this point of our investigation. We have *a lot* of work to do back in town.
Zh: “好吧。但是在那之后，我们要继续自己的任务。调查进行到这个阶段，像这样绕道而行真的找不到任何合理的解释。而且我们在城里还有*很多*工作要做。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Okay. But after that we get back to our own assignments. We've done a thorough survey of the coast now -- just running around is no longer useful.
Zh: “好吧。但是在那之后，我们要继续自己的任务。既然现在已经对海岸进行了十分彻底的搜查——再这样到处跑来跑去就没什么用了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Good, good. We should be courteous and tell the cryptozoologists that one of their traps was empty, but then we can get back to *our* assignment here.
Zh: “很好，很好。我们应该礼貌一点，告诉神秘动物学家有一个陷阱空了，然后就继续回去完成*我们自己*的任务。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: He purses his lips. "Perhaps our cryptozoologists have competition in the form of an actual entomologist? Or someone else is sabotaging them? I could present more theories, but then I would be taking this on as a case -- which I'm *not*."
Zh: 他咬起嘴唇。“也许我们的神秘动物学家与真正的昆虫学家之间存在竞争关系？又或者其他什么人在蓄意破坏？我可以提出更多的理论，不过那样的话，可能就要立案了——但我并*不想*这么做。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Another empty trap, the lieutenant takes a note -- more out of habit than duty.
Zh: “又是个空陷阱，”警督记下笔记——相比职责，更像是出于一种习惯。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: He stops you. "Don't answer that. It was a rhetorical question."
Zh: 他拦住了你。“别回答。这是个反问句。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Let's keep going. The next one is the *lucky* one.
Zh: “我们走吧。下一个应该就能*走运*了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: How are you enjoying the cardio, lieutenant? I'm quite enjoying it myself.
Zh: “你喜欢有氧运动吗，警督？我自己觉得挺舒服的。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Again -- I want to make it absolutely *clear* that I don't really believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “再说一次——我要明确*声明*一点，我其实并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: I want to make it clear that I don't *actually* believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “我想声明一点，我*其实*并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Yup. The next one has a *crab* trapped in it. He raises his collar -- it's windy. "These are just crab traps, you do realize?"
Zh: “是的。下一个里面困了只*螃蟹*。”他竖起衣领——起风了。“你有没有发现，这些只不过是抓螃蟹的陷阱？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Always up for a good jog -- otherwise, would I still be *on* this case with *you*? He smiles and raises his collar. It's windy.
Zh: “我一直好好坚持早起慢跑——不然的话，我还会跟*你*一起破*这个*案子吗？”他笑着竖起了衣领，突然起风了。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Neither does the lieutenant. He just raises his collar -- it's cold out, as always.
Zh: 警督也没有。他只是竖起衣领——毕竟外面很冷，一如往常。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Man, it's lonely doing this alone. The wind seems to howl in a strange manner...
Zh: 伙计，一个人做这个也太孤独了。风似乎都在以一种奇怪的方式呼啸……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: All the traps are stocked now -- and still no sight of the phasmid. Return to Morell and let him know.
Zh: 现在所有陷阱都设置好了——但还是没有看见竹节虫。回到莫雷尔那里，把消息告诉他。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Actually -- I do get the feeling that someone or something may have messed with the trap...
Zh: “其实——我确实感觉到好像有什么人，或者什么东西在破坏陷阱……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Yeah, it probably *wasn't* the phasmid, but still. Morell needs to know.
Zh: “是啊，它也许*不是*竹节虫，不过莫雷尔还是需要知道这一点。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: But what if it *was* the phasmid? What if it ate them and got out?
Zh: “但是如果*真是*竹节虫呢？如果它把蝗虫全吃掉，然后跑出来了呢？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: You're right. But they still need to know.
Zh: “你说得对。不过他们还是需要知道。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: You're right. This crypto-research has been stupid.
Zh: “你说得对。这次的神秘动物学研究实在是太愚蠢了。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds continue to hiss around you as you put the empty trap back on the ground. Time to consult the professionals -- Morell and Lena.
Zh: 芦苇不断地在你周围嘶嘶作响，你把空陷阱又放回到地面上。是时候去找专业人士咨询一下了——莫雷尔和莉娜。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Damn, I was hoping it would be in the *first* one.
Zh: “该死，我还希望这会是*第一*个呢。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Empty as all of them... he pants. "*One* more of these and we're done..." His face is red from the cold sea air. He crouches to catch his breath.
Zh: “跟其他的一样空……”他喘着气。“最多再看*一个*，我们就不做了……”他的脸被冰冷的海风吹得通红，蹲伏下身体喘着气。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Surely, he repeats and looks at the sea, then at you putting the trap back on the ground. "Anyway, the air is nice and fresh here..."
Zh: “当然了，”他望着大海又重复了一遍，接着看着你把陷阱放回地面上。“不管怎么说，这里的空气不错，很新鲜……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: It'll be in the next one. *Surely*.
Zh: “肯定在下一个陷阱里面。*绝对的*。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Bummer it wasn't in here...
Zh: “可惜，它不在这里……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: You getting tired?
Zh: “你累了吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: One more time, I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*再强调一遍，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*强调一下，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The lieutenant stretches his back.
Zh: 警督挺直后背。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: I'm just... he breathes out. "Glad we haven't found some poor cat trapped in one of these."
Zh: “我只是……”他吐出一口气。“很高兴没在里面找到被困住的可怜小猫。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: No you weren't. He looks at the sea, then you -- laying the trap back down on the ground.
Zh: “不，你不会的。”他看着大海，然后看着你——把陷阱放回地面。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: They're not really going to get the chance to get comfortable here.
Zh: 它们舒舒服服地生活在这里的机会基本为零。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Good. Now that's done. When do you think we will return to our impending apocalypse of a murder investigation?
Zh: “很好。现在这个事情已经告一段落。我们什么时候才能看到凶杀案调查的启示呢？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Release the locusts into the empty trap.
Zh: 把蝗虫放进空陷阱。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: (Immediately yell.) "It's empty! Morell didn't leave it empty..."
Zh: （立马大叫出来。）“它是空的！莫雷尔留下它的时候可不是空的……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Nothing. No traces of the locusts *or* the Insulindian phasmid. What happened?
Zh: 什么也没有。没有蝗虫*或者*是伊苏林迪竹节虫的痕迹。出什么事了？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Look closer still.
Zh: 靠近查看。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: No phasmid either -- but still...
Zh: 也没有竹节虫——不过……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Locusts are crawling around in the trap, confused but uneaten. You see no carnivorous *reed-phasmid* gorging on them.
Zh: 蝗虫在陷阱里爬行，没有被吃掉，但却十分迷惑。你没看见食肉的*芦苇-竹节虫*在狼吞虎咽地享用它们。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: I meant no offence, just... The lieutenant doesn't know how to finish the sentence. He looks at you, putting the trap back on the ground.
Zh: “我不想冒犯，只是……”警督不知道该如何说完这句话。他看着你，然后把陷阱放回地面。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The locusts, dazed from being transported, slowly begin to acclimate to their new surroundings.
Zh: 刚经历了长途跋涉的蝗虫还有些头晕，不过也开始慢慢地适应起周围的新环境来。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Big surprise. The lieutenant grins mirthlessly. "Anyway -- one down, three to go."
Zh: “大惊喜。”警督阴沉地咧嘴笑了。“不管怎么说——排除一个，还剩三个。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the empty trap. That one was by the canal.
Zh: 蝗虫的状况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这个陷阱不是空的。那个在运河边。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the empty trap. That one Morell was setting up, near the cryptozoologists camp.
Zh: 蝗虫的状况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这个陷阱不是空的。那个是莫雷尔设置的，就在神秘动物学家的营地旁边。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the empty trap. That one was in Land's End, in the northern tip of this peninsula.
Zh: 蝗虫的状况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这个陷阱不是空的。那个在地之角，这座半岛的最北端。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The locusts aren't doing all too well, but they're still in there. This isn't the empty trap. That must have been another one.
Zh: 蝗虫的情况不太好，不过它们还在里面。这个陷阱不是空的。肯定还有其他的。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: It's good the cryptozoologists left -- this isn't a very cosy place to stay night after night.
Zh: 还好神秘动物学家离开了——在这里过夜可不舒服。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds by the abandoned camp site hiss and shake in the lazily falling rain.
Zh: 雨滴洋洋洒洒地飘落，废弃营地旁的芦苇沙沙地摇晃着。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds by the abandoned camp site sway and tremble, while the snow falls all around.
Zh: 雪花从空中飘落，废弃营地旁的芦苇摇摆着、颤抖着。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds hiss and shake in the darkness that has settled over the abandoned camp.
Zh: 黑暗笼罩着这片废弃的营地，芦苇沙沙地颤抖着。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds by the abandoned camp site hiss and shake.
Zh: 废弃营地旁的芦苇沙沙地摇晃着。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The netting is a little untidy, messier than the others. Like someone or something picked up the trap and shook it before dropping it back down on the ground.
Zh: 相比其他陷阱，这个的网更凌乱一些。就好像有人或者什么东西把陷阱捡了起来，摇了摇，然后又丢回地面。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The later it gets, the colder. Remnants of the camp can still be seen in the sand, the fire that's gone out... you feel strange, somehow.
Zh: 天色越晚，温度就越低。露营地残留下来的东西还散落在沙子里，火已经熄灭了……不知为什么，你感觉很奇怪。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds whisper around the sepulchral trap, as though suddenly filled with fear. Even the snow does not quiet them.
Zh: 芦苇围绕在阴森森的陷阱旁窃窃私语，好像突然被恐惧包围一样。就连雪花也无法给它们带去安慰。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds shudder in the falling rain, whispering. It is as though the sepulchral trap has filled them with some strange, novel fear.
Zh: 芦苇在落雨中颤抖着，低语着。仿佛这个阴森森的陷阱让它们产生了某种奇怪而诡异的恐惧。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The reeds whisper around the sepulchral trap, as though suddenly filled with fear.
Zh: 芦苇围绕在阴森森的陷阱旁窃窃私语，好像突然被恐惧包围一样。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: A few locusts trudge along the wall of the trap. The rest are piled in a heap in the corner, dead. No phasmid anywhere.
Zh: 一群蝗虫沿着陷阱的内壁艰难地跋涉着。其余的挤在一个角落，已经死去了。到处都没有竹节虫的踪影。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Yes. Like a cat, or another teenage psychopath.
Zh: “是的。比如说一只猫，或者是又一个精神变态的青年。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: I'm beginning to feel... somewhat sorry for them.
Zh: “我开始有点……为它们感到抱歉。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Poor things...
Zh: 可怜的家伙……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Pause and just stand there.
Zh: 停下来，只是静静站着。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: Let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧。”[离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The trap stands empty near the reeds, powdered with snow. No insect sounds or movement around -- only the reeds' apprehensive hissing.
Zh: 空空如也的陷阱站立在芦苇附近，上面布满雪花。周围没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作——只有芦苇那忧郁的沙沙声。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The trap stands empty near the reeds. No insect sounds or movement around -- only the reeds' apprehensive hissing. Rain drips from the  mesh of the trap.
Zh: 空空如也的陷阱站立在芦苇附近。周围没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作——只有芦苇那忧郁的沙沙声。雨点拍打在陷阱的网格上。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds by camp site
En: The trap stands empty near the reeds. No insect sounds or movement around -- only the reeds' apprehensive hissing.
Zh: 空空如也的陷阱站立在芦苇附近。周围没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作——只有芦苇那忧郁的沙沙声。

En: Reach for the trap.
Zh: 伸手去碰陷阱。

En: Look around.
Zh: 看看周围。

En: The reeds rustle confidentially, while the residential building, menacing in the darkness behind you, shield them from the wind.
Zh: 一栋居民楼恶狠狠地站在你身后的黑影里，保护芦苇不受大风侵袭，而芦苇则回以信任的沙沙声。

En: Behind you, a ruined residential building looms over the reeds, shielding them from the wind. They rustle confidentially, in tune with the pitter-patter of the rain.
Zh: 你身后是一栋居民楼的废墟，在芦苇丛中若隐若现，保护着它们不受大风的侵袭。芦苇回以信任的沙沙声，与噼里啪啦的雨声格外合拍。

En: Behind you, the ruins of a residential building loom over the reeds. They whisper amongst themselves confidentially. Snowflakes cling to their shivering stems.
Zh: 你身后是一栋居民楼的废墟，在芦苇丛中若隐若现，悄悄同它们低声慢语。从天而降的雪花，贴服在芦苇颤抖的茎杆上。

En: Behind you, the ruins of a residential building rise over the reeds, shielding them from the wind. The reeds rustle confidentially.
Zh: 你身后是一栋居民楼的废墟，高高地耸立在芦苇丛上，保护它们不受大风侵袭，而芦苇回以信任的沙沙声。

En: Big surprise. The lieutenant grins mirthlessly. "Anyway -- one down, three to go."
Zh: “大惊喜。”警督阴沉地咧嘴笑了。“不管怎么说——排除一个，还剩三个。”

En: When this district was booming, the reeds were kept at bay. Nothing obscured the freshly painted façades, nowhere for drunks and adventurous teenagers to hide. Now only the wind blows...
Zh: 这片区域飞速发展的时候，芦苇只能在海湾边生长。新刷的墙面没有任何东西遮挡，让酒鬼和爱冒险的青少年无处可藏。现在只剩下风在呼啸……

En: Damn, I was hoping it would be in the *first* one.
Zh: “该死，我还希望这会是*第一*个呢。”

En: No need to grin. I'm not *expecting* to find anything. I'm helping some citizens and getting some fresh air.
Zh: “不用笑的这么勉强。我没*期望*能找到什么。我只是在帮助市民，然后顺便呼吸一下新鲜空气。”

En: It'll be in the next one. *Surely*.
Zh: “肯定在下一个陷阱里面。*绝对的*。”

En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

En: No you weren't. He looks at the sea, then you -- laying the trap back down on the ground.
Zh: “不，你不会的。”他看着大海，然后看着你——把陷阱放回地面。

En: I meant no offence, just... The lieutenant doesn't know how to finish the sentence. He looks at you, putting the trap back on the ground.
Zh: “我不想冒犯，只是……”警督不知道该如何说完这句话。他看着你，然后把陷阱放回地面。

En: Surely, he repeats and looks at the sea, then at you putting the trap back on the ground. "Anyway, the air is nice and fresh here..."
Zh: “当然了，”他望着大海又重复了一遍，接着看着你把陷阱放回地面上。“不管怎么说，这里的空气不错，很新鲜……”

En: You set the apparatus back on the ground, among the reeds.
Zh: 你把装置放回地面，隐藏在芦苇丛中。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: There's a trap in the reeds at your feet. Looks like the same one you saw Morell set before -- same mesh, same wiring.
Zh: 你脚下的芦苇丛里有一个陷阱。跟之前看见的莫雷尔的陷阱一模一样——同样的网，同样的线。

En: A familiar apparatus lies among the reeds -- another one of Morell's traps, weighed down by stones to keep it in place.
Zh: 一个熟悉的装置躺在芦苇丛中……又是莫雷尔的陷阱，上面压着石头，方便固定它的位置。

En: This trap's not too hard to spot -- once you know what to look for. Keeping it hidden has not been a priority for the cryptozoologist.
Zh: 要找到这个陷阱并不太难——只要你明白自己在找什么。对于神秘动物学家来说，把它隐藏起来可不是最高优先级。

En: It takes you a moment, but finally you spot the last of Morell's traps. This one's partially obscured by the reeds...
Zh: 你花了一会儿功夫，总算是找到了莫雷尔的最后一个陷阱。有一部分被芦苇给挡住了……

En: Nothing has changed about this trap. You need to check the others.
Zh: 这个陷阱没有任何变化。你需要检查一下其它的。

En: The trap feels light -- and silent -- as you pick it up. Something is different here...
Zh: 你捡起这个陷阱，感觉它很轻——还很安静。好像有点不太一样……

En: Locusts are crawling around in the trap, confused but uneaten. You see no carnivorous *reed-phasmid* gorging on them.
Zh: 蝗虫在陷阱里爬行，没有被吃掉，但却十分迷惑。你没看见食肉的*芦苇-竹节虫*在狼吞虎咽地享用它们。

En: This trap is also full of panicked locusts. No sign of any cryptozoological beast inside.
Zh: 这个陷阱里面也满是惊慌失措的蝗虫，没有任何神秘动物学范畴的野兽存在。

En: Nothing but locusts in this trap as well. Definitely no cryptozoological monstrosity.
Zh: 这个陷阱也一样，除了蝗虫什么也没有。肯定没有神秘动物学意义上的畸形。

En: No locusts!
Zh: 没有蝗虫！

En: Look closer.
Zh: 再仔细看看。

En: Another empty trap, the lieutenant takes a note -- more out of habit than duty.
Zh: “又是个空陷阱，”警督记下笔记——相比职责，更像是出于一种习惯。

En: Let's keep going. The next one is the *lucky* one.
Zh: “我们走吧。下一个应该就能*走运*了。”

En: How are you enjoying the cardio, lieutenant? I'm quite enjoying it myself.
Zh: “你喜欢有氧运动吗，警督？我自己觉得挺舒服的。”

En: Again -- I want to make it absolutely *clear* that I don't really believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “再说一次——我要明确*声明*一点，我其实并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

En: I want to make it clear that I don't *actually* believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “我想声明一点，我*其实*并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

En: Yup. The next one has a *crab* trapped in it. He raises his collar -- it's windy. "These are just crab traps, you do realize?"
Zh: “是的。下一个里面困了只*螃蟹*。”他竖起衣领——起风了。“你有没有发现，这些只不过是抓螃蟹的陷阱？”

En: Always up for a good jog -- otherwise, would I still be *on* this case with *you*? He smiles and raises his collar. It's windy.
Zh: “我一直好好坚持早起慢跑——不然的话，我还会跟*你*一起破*这个*案子吗？”他笑着竖起了衣领，突然起风了。

En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

En: Neither does the lieutenant. He just raises his collar -- it's cold out, as always.
Zh: 警督也没有。他只是竖起衣领——毕竟外面很冷，一如往常。

En: Man, it's lonely doing this alone. The wind seems to howl in a strange manner...
Zh: 伙计，一个人做这个也太孤独了。风似乎都在以一种奇怪的方式呼啸……

En: Empty as all of them... he pants. "*One* more of these and we're done..." His face is red from the cold sea air. He crouches to catch his breath.
Zh: “跟其他的一样空……”他喘着气。“最多再看*一个*，我们就不做了……”他的脸被冰冷的海风吹得通红，蹲伏下身体喘着气。

En: Bummer it wasn't in here...
Zh: “可惜，它不在这里……”

En: You getting tired?
Zh: “你累了吗？”

En: One more time, I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*再强调一遍，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

En: I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*强调一下，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

En: Say nothing, just put the trap down.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是把陷阱放下。

En: The lieutenant stretches his back.
Zh: 警督挺直后背。

En: I'm just... he breathes out. "Glad we haven't found some poor cat trapped in one of these."
Zh: “我只是……”他吐出一口气。“很高兴没在里面找到被困住的可怜小猫。”

En: No-no. I'm fine. I didn't mean to complain, it's just... He's short-winded. The sentence ends there.
Zh: “不。我很好。我不是想抱怨，只不过……”他有些气喘。话语到这里就结束了。

En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

En: (Immediately yell.) "It's empty!"
Zh: （立马大叫出来。）“它是空的！”

En: The lieutenant studies the trap with you. "Well, the bait worked on *something*. This doesn't mean it was a reed-monster, though. Unless you see one in there? I just see an empty trap..."
Zh: 警督跟你一起研究陷阱。“呃，诱饵对*某种东西*起作用了。但也并不意味着它就是芦苇怪兽。除非你亲眼看见里面有？我只看到一个空陷阱……”

En: Nothing. No traces of the locusts *or* the Insulindian phasmid. What happened?
Zh: 什么也没有。没有蝗虫*或者*是伊苏林迪竹节虫的痕迹。出什么事了？

En: Look closer still.
Zh: 靠近查看。

En: The reeds continue to hiss around you as you put the empty trap back on the ground. Time to consult the professionals -- Morell and Lena.
Zh: 芦苇不断地在你周围嘶嘶作响，你把空陷阱又放回到地面上。是时候去找专业人士咨询一下了——莫雷尔和莉娜。

En: He purses his lips. "Perhaps our cryptozoologists have competition in the form of an actual entomologist? Or someone else is sabotaging them? I could present more theories, but then I would be taking this on as a case -- which I'm *not*."
Zh: 他咬起嘴唇。“也许我们的神秘动物学家与真正的昆虫学家之间存在竞争关系？又或者其他什么人在蓄意破坏？我可以提出更多的理论，不过那样的话，可能就要立案了——但我并*不想*这么做。”

En: No phasmid either -- but still...
Zh: 也没有竹节虫——不过……

En: Your voice echoes on the coast, carried by a gust of cold wind.
Zh: 一阵冷风卷起你的声音，在海岸边发出回响。

En: You're right. This crypto-research has been stupid.
Zh: “你说得对。这次的神秘动物学研究实在是太愚蠢了。”

En: You're right. But I still need to tell the cryptozoologists about this.
Zh: “你说得对。但我还是要跟神秘动物学家说一声。”

En: But what if it *was* the phasmid? What if it ate them and got out?
Zh: “但是如果*真是*竹节虫呢？如果它把蝗虫全吃掉，然后跑出来了呢？”

En: Good, good. We should be courteous and tell the cryptozoologists that one of their traps was empty, but then we can get back to *our* assignment here.
Zh: “很好，很好。我们应该礼貌一点，告诉神秘动物学家有一个陷阱空了，然后就继续回去完成*我们自己*的任务。”

En: Yeah, it probably *wasn't* the phasmid, but still. Morell needs to know.
Zh: “是啊，它也许*不是*竹节虫，不过莫雷尔还是需要知道这一点。”

Zh: “我们确实*好像*答应过要告诉他们，是吧……”他好像为这个事实感到有些后悔。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: All the traps are stocked now -- and still no sight of the phasmid. Return to Morell and let him know.
Zh: 现在所有陷阱都设置好了——但还是没有看见竹节虫。回到莫雷尔那里，把消息告诉他。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car

Zh: “不。我很好。我不是想抱怨，只不过……”他有些气喘。话语到这里就结束了。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: You getting tired?
Zh: “你累了吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: A cold gust of air dries your sweaty face and you look to the dark shadow the Feld building in the distance, drawing you to it. What a strange sensation... Once this is done, should you ask the wind again?
Zh: 一阵冷风吹干你脸上的汗水，你看着远处菲尔德大厦的黑色阴影，慢慢被它吸引。多么奇怪的一种感觉……等这些结束之后，你再问问风？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
