Zh: 他拦住了你。“别回答。这是个反问句。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: One more time, I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*再强调一遍，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: I *must* stress that I did not expect a cryptozoological monstrosity to be in this trap.
Zh: “我*必须*强调一下，我可没期望能在这个陷阱里看到神秘动物学范畴的畸形。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: He doesn't want to, but if there is one more cryptozoological run-around, he *must* force the investigation back on track. This better be it...
Zh: 他不想这么做，但是如果再来一次神秘动物学的借口，他*肯定*会强迫调查回归正轨。最好就到此为止了……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Man, it's lonely doing this alone. The wind seems to howl in a strange manner...
Zh: 伙计，一个人做这个也太孤独了。风似乎都在以一种奇怪的方式呼啸……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: There's nothing else to do with the traps at this time. They're all just lying around, gathering dust and rainwater.
Zh: 现在已经没什么可以做的了。陷阱只是躺在那里，收集灰尘和雨水。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: You've checked all the traps now. There's nothing else to do with them. The cryptozoologists await your report.
Zh: 现在所有陷阱已经检查完毕。没什么需要做的了。神秘动物学家还在等待你的报告。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Empty as all of them... he pants. "*One* more of these and we're done..." His face is red from the cold sea air. He crouches to catch his breath.
Zh: “跟其他的一样空……”他喘着气。“最多再看*一个*，我们就不做了……”他的脸被冰冷的海风吹得通红，蹲伏下身体喘着气。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Of course. Noted. He looks at you, incredulously, as you place the trap back in the reeds.
Zh: “当然。记下来了。”他怀疑地看着你把陷阱放回到芦苇丛中。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Always up for a good jog -- otherwise, would I still be *on* this case with *you*? He smiles and raises his collar. It's windy.
Zh: “我一直好好坚持早起慢跑——不然的话，我还会跟*你*一起破*这个*案子吗？”他笑着竖起了衣领，突然起风了。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Another empty trap, the lieutenant takes a note -- more out of habit than duty.
Zh: “又是个空陷阱，”警督记下笔记——相比职责，更像是出于一种习惯。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Nothing. No traces of the locusts *or* the Insulindian phasmid. What happened?
Zh: 什么也没有。没有蝗虫*或者*是伊苏林迪竹节虫的痕迹。出什么事了？

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: This trap's not too hard to spot -- once you know what to look for. Keeping it hidden has not been a priority for the cryptozoologist.
Zh: 要找到这个陷阱并不太难——只要你明白自己在找什么。对于神秘动物学家来说，把它隐藏起来可不是最高优先级。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Again -- I want to make it absolutely *clear* that I don't really believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “再说一次——我要明确*声明*一点，我其实并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: I want to make it clear that I don't *actually* believe the phasmid exists, okay?
Zh: “我想声明一点，我*其实*并不相信竹节虫的存在，好吗？”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: How are you enjoying the cardio, lieutenant? I'm quite enjoying it myself.
Zh: “你喜欢有氧运动吗，警督？我自己觉得挺舒服的。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Reach for the trap.
Zh: 伸手去碰陷阱。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: This trap is also full of panicked locusts. No sign of any cryptozoological beast inside.
Zh: 这个陷阱里面也满是惊慌失措的蝗虫，没有任何神秘动物学范畴的野兽存在。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Nothing but locusts in this trap as well. Definitely no cryptozoological monstrosity.
Zh: 这个陷阱也一样，除了蝗虫什么也没有。肯定没有神秘动物学意义上的畸形。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: There's a trap in the reeds at your feet. Looks like the same one you saw Morell set before -- same mesh, same wiring.
Zh: 你脚下的芦苇丛里有一个陷阱。跟之前看见的莫雷尔的陷阱一模一样——同样的网，同样的线。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Bummer it wasn't in here...
Zh: “可惜，它不在这里……”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: I'm just... he breathes out. "Glad we haven't found some poor cat trapped in one of these."
Zh: “我只是……”他吐出一口气。“很高兴没在里面找到被困住的可怜小猫。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The trap stands empty amongst the reeds, powdered with snow. No insect sounds or movement around -- only the reeds' melancholy rustling.
Zh: 空空如也的陷阱站立在芦苇丛中，上面堆满雪花。周围没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作——只有芦苇那忧郁的沙沙声。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The trap stands empty amongst the reeds. No insect sounds or movement around -- only the reeds' melancholy rustling. Rain drips from the netting of the trap.
Zh: 空空如也的陷阱站立在芦苇丛中。周围没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作——只有芦苇那忧郁的沙沙声。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The trap stands empty amongst the reeds. No insect sounds or movement around -- only the reeds' melancholy rustling.
Zh: 空空如也的陷阱站立在芦苇丛中。周围没有昆虫的声音，或者是动作——只有芦苇那忧郁的沙沙声。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The constant, distant song. Louder on this part of the coast. Nearer somehow. And there's that cold again, always the cold...
Zh: 那遥远的，不变的歌声。在海岸的这一部分尤其响亮。不知为什么，好像更近一些。还有那刺骨的寒冷，永远不变的寒冷……

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Look around.
Zh: 看看周围。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The reeds bend forlornly toward the sand. Some tufts have been crushed. The broken stalks seem like a rebuke -- the sound of the city centre hums in the east.
Zh: 芦苇孤苦伶仃地弯向沙地，有几丛已经被碾碎，折断的茎杆就像一场责罚。在东边，城市中心发出嗡嗡的声响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The reeds bend forlornly toward the sand. Snow covers the broken stalks like a shroud, and they shimmer, ghostly, in the darkness. In the east, the city centre hums to you.
Zh: 芦苇孤苦伶仃地弯向沙地。雪花像寿衣一样盖在折断的茎杆上，它们在黑暗中颤抖地，仿佛幽灵一样。在东边，市中心朝着你嗡嗡作响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The reeds bend forlornly toward the sand. Some tufts have been crushed. Snow covers the broken stalks like a shroud. In the east, the city centre hums to you.
Zh: 芦苇孤苦伶仃地弯向沙地，有几丛已经被碾碎。雪花像寿衣一样盖在折断的茎杆上。在东边，市中心朝着你嗡嗡作响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The reeds bend forlornly toward the water. Some tufts have been crushed. The broken stalks, sickly-pale against the darkness. In the east, the city centre hums to you.
Zh: 芦苇孤苦伶仃地弯向沙地，有几丛已经被碾碎。折断的茎杆，在黑暗中愈发显现出病态般的苍白。在东边，市中心朝着你嗡嗡作响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: The reeds bend forlornly toward the sand. You see slabs of concrete, north. In the east, the city centre hums to you.
Zh: 芦苇孤苦伶仃地弯向沙地。在北边，你望见混凝土石板。在东边，市中心朝着你嗡嗡作响。

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: This was the one.
Zh: “就是这个。”

ConvTitle: COAST ORB / reeds near sunken car
En: Fantastic. The lieutenant looks at the familiar contraption and shrugs.
Zh: “太棒了。”警督看着眼熟的精妙装置，耸了耸肩。

En: That patch of reeds over there -- it's a great place to hide something. Kind of out of the way, being so close to the water.
Zh: 那边的芦苇丛——是藏东西的好地方。那么靠近水边，有点不同寻常。

En: Wait, I can't see anything interesting...
Zh: 等等，我没看见什么有意思的东西……

En: You don't have a reason to -- yet.
Zh: 你没有理由——暂时没有。

En: What is this about?
Zh: 这是干什么？

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: Nothing. Just a hunch. The hunch passes, leaving you there, by the old buoy bobbing in the water. Time to go.
Zh: 没什么。只是有种预感。预感逝去，把你独自留在这里——水面若隐若现的旧浮标旁。时间差不多，该走了。

En: Just me, I guess?
Zh: 我猜，只有我？

En: Some nasty water?
Zh: 脏水？

En: This is more important than you. That's the blood of industry you see before you. The run off from Coal City, further down the coast.
Zh: 这远比你重要。出现在你眼前的是工业的血液。从海岸那头遥远的煤城流出来的。

En: No! For a second, think about something other than yourself -- think about *the blood of industry* here before you. The run off from Coal City, further down the coast.
Zh: 不！只稍片刻，想想除你以外的东西——想想你眼前的*工业的血液*。从海岸那头遥远的煤城流出来的。

En: Exactly! The *blood of industry*. The run off from Coal City, further down the coast.
Zh: 没错！*工业的血液*。从海岸那头遥远的煤城流出来的。

En: Metaphor. It's always metaphor.
Zh: 隐喻。一直都是隐喻。

En: What happened?
Zh: 出什么事了？

En: Wait -- take a longer look at yourself and how you're reflected in that slick chemical rainbow. What do you *see*?
Zh: 等等——好好看看你自己，看看你在那道光滑的化学彩虹下的倒影。你*看见*什么了？

En: Some kind of metaphor for me?
Zh: 对我来说是某种隐喻吗？

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: The engines of fortune once roared here. Great wealth poured into Revachol, the Delta. As did smoke, waste, sickness, life...
Zh: 财富的引擎曾经在这里咆哮。大量财富涌入瑞瓦肖，德尔塔，同时还有烟雾、废品、疾病和生命……

En: The engines stopped. The West Revacholian industrial base was dismantled after the war. Now, extinguished coke furnaces dot the landscape. A landscape despoiled by industry.
Zh: 引擎已经停止。战后，西瑞瓦肖工业基地被拆除了。现在，熄灭的焦炭炉点缀着这片风景。被工业掠夺过的风景。

En: I don't see the problem. Revachol moved on to a service industry. Get with the times, yo.
Zh: 我没觉得有什么问题。瑞瓦肖转向了服务业。要跟上时代嘛。

En: There was a serious dereliction of duty in this clean up.
Zh: 清理工作中存在着严重的失职行为。

En: Right. Here's some advice that's good for business and life: never fight a losing battle. I'm not even going to argue with you.
Zh: 没错。这里有一些对生意和生活都很有益的建议：永远都不要打败仗。我甚至都不想跟你争辩。

En: Filth! This needs environmental action -- now!
Zh: 肮脏！这里需要环境行动——就是现在！

En: Perhaps you could dedicate your life to cleaning it up. But you're a cop. You must concern yourself with different messes. Even so: it's pretty to think.
Zh: 也许你可以奉献自己的一生来清理它。但你是个警察。你还有其他不同的烂摊子要考虑。即便如此：想想还是挺好的。

En: West Revacholian industrial base? That's so me.
Zh: 西瑞瓦肖工业基地？听起来很适合我。

En: Yes. Fortune left you too, leaving only pharmaceuticals and a sub-par service based industry.
Zh: 是的。财富也离开了你，只留下制药和服务水平低下的工业。

En: His boyfriend is on his way home. He brings tins of meat and vegetables with him. Their pockets are heavier with money; but only slightly.
Zh: 他的男朋友还在回家路上，身上带着肉类和蔬菜罐头。他们的口袋因为金钱变得沉重了些；不过只有一点而已。

En: A drop in temperature. An easy flow of air: an empty street. Before you, a thoroughfare unjammed with lorries. No more drivers smoking on hitch steps. Just... silence.
Zh: 温度骤降。在一条空旷的街道上，连空气的流动也变得容易起来。在你面前，一条没有货車堵死的大道。没有坐在托杆上抽烟的司机。只有……寂静。

En: Let go of the moment. [Discard thought.]
Zh: 放下这一刻。[放弃思维。]

En: What did the smoke smell like?
Zh: 烟是什么味道？

En: Chemically sweetened. Across the road, a forgotten bus stop; corrosion has opened a hole in its roof. An elm tree watches over the building. Its branches are dripping with rain and snow.
Zh: 化学的甜味。道路那边是一个被遗忘的公交車站；锈迹在它的顶部开了个洞。一棵榆树看守着建筑。它的枝头滴落着雨水和雪水。

En: The road is smooth and motley. Craters filled with a black asphalt. The asphalt first laid is grey already. A row of tenements are under construction in the distance.
Zh: 道路通畅而混杂。坑洞里填满黑色的沥青。第一次铺设的沥青已经变灰。一排建造中的公寓伫立在远方。

En: What about the bus stop?
Zh: 那个公交車站呢？

En: That is enough. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 已经够了。[完成思维。]

En: Who are the people who live across the road?
Zh: 住在路对面的都是什么人？

En: A dead dog?
Zh: 一条死狗？

En: A tub warm with water, white with soap. A man bathes while radio waves transmit the lottery numbers: 4, 18, 21, 4, 1... A modern washing machine rattles a drawer full of silverware.
Zh: 一盆温水，上面漂浮着白色的肥皂沫。一个男人正在洗澡，无线电波正里传输着彩票中奖号码：4,18,21,4,1……洗碗机把装满镀银餐具的抽屉晃得咯咯直响。

En: #312D. Young girls used to come here, huddled up, hoping for more warmth than their thin coats give them. The bus took them to school. It has not run for eight years; there were not enough girls to sustain its cost.
Zh: 312D号。以前会有年轻的女孩来到这里，挤成一团，寻求着比身上单薄大衣更多的温暖。公交車把她们送往学校。它已经有8年没有运行了；没有足够多的女孩来负担它运营的成本。

En: There's one bump on the road: a dead dog lies flat about two hundred paces away, right at the turn.
Zh: 路上有一块隆起：一条死狗平躺在两百米开外，刚好就在转角的地方。

En: Craters pocked the surface. Children played in them, until heavy trucks full of black pitch rolled in. The landowners have filled the craters with money. It is a vital artery of flow of trade.
Zh: 路的表面密密麻麻全是坑洞。孩子们在里面玩耍，直到载满黑色沥青的重型货車蜂拥而至。土地所有者用钱填满了这些坑洞。它是商业流通的重要命脉。

En: Tragedy came from the wheels of a fast RCM vehicle, hurrying to work. The cold washes over you. The sound of the sea has grown distant.
Zh: 悲剧来自于飞驰的車轮，那是急着赶去工作的RCM专車。一股寒意充斥着你的全身。大海的声音渐行渐远。

En: The wind moves the aerosol. A detective stands behind the boom barrier; a breeze moves a curl of his hair.
Zh: 风吹散了气溶胶。一个警探站在拦車杆的后面；一阵微风吹起了他的一缕卷发。

En: What about the road?
Zh: 那条路呢？

En: You see that seagull up there? Remind you of anybody?
Zh: 你看见上面那只海鸥了吗？有没有让你想起谁？

En: Um... me?
Zh: 呃……我？

En: Kim?
Zh: 金？

En: What? Kim? No, it's you! You! You and the seagull are just alike!
Zh: 什么？金？不，是你！你！你和海鸥太像了！

En: Exactly!
Zh: 没错！

En: Why am I like a seagull?
Zh: 为什么我像海鸥？

En: Think about the seagull's story. It's one of endurance -- and adaptation. The seaside was paradise once. They were birds of that paradise.
Zh: 想想海鸥的故事吧。那是关于忍耐力——还有适应性的故事。海边曾经是天堂般的存在，鸟儿们的天堂。

En: No time for that sentimental bullshit. They're hustlers, getting shit done. They're one pair of track pants away from gangsters. Just like you.
Zh: 没时间搞那些多愁善感的狗屁东西了。它们是骗子，专门做些恶心的事。它们离歹徒只有一步之遥。就像你一样。

En: I guess I *am* a bit like the seagull.
Zh: 我猜我*的确*有一点像海鸥。

En: But I'm not like that! I'm still romantic.
Zh: 但我一点都不像啊！我还是很浪漫的。

En: Sure, *inside*. But outside it's a tough world. You've got to do whatever it takes to survive. And you *do*. Question is: will you admit that to yourself?
Zh: 当然，*内在*是。不过外面是一个艰难的世界。为了生存，你什么都要做。而且你的确*做了*。问题是：你自己会承认吗？

En: Exactly. The seagull does what it takes -- and so do you. You've got that same spirit in you. When the time comes, you'll push yourself.
Zh: 没错。海鸥会竭尽所能——就像你一样。你也拥有同样的精神。当时机到来的时候，你会鞭策自己的。

En: Fuckin' right. Whatever it takes to survive. I AM THE SEAGULL!
Zh: 没错。尽一切可能活下来。我就是海鸥！

En: Own it! Steal hot dogs, shit in the sand -- whatever it takes to keep going!
Zh: 拥抱它吧！偷走热狗，在沙子里大便——无论如何也要继续坚持下去！

En: Okay... well, let me re-consider.
Zh: 好吧……呃，让我重新考虑一下。

En: I will not, no. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 我不会的。[完成思维。]

En: I'm not going to have a seagull conversation. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 我不想聊海鸥的话题。[完成思维。]

En: No one comes to mind.
Zh: 没想到什么人。

En: You! You and the seagull are just alike!
Zh: 你啊！你和海鸥太像了！

En: Then their paradise became *shit city*. And what did they do? They became urban survivors! Eating burgers out of trash cans! Killing and eating pigeons!
Zh: 然后它们的天堂变成了*该死的城市*。它们做了什么？它们变成了城市的幸存者！吃着垃圾箱里的汉堡！杀掉鸽子，然后把它们吃掉！

En: Good boy.
Zh: 好孩子。

En: Hold on. Have I ever eaten a burger out of the trash?
Zh: 等一等。我有没有在垃圾箱翻过汉堡吃？

En: You have. The body remembers.
Zh: 你有过。你的身体还记得。

En: The snow falls lazily, making the beach sand paler still, mixing with the rust-coloured sewage run-off.
Zh: 雪花洋洋洒洒地飘落，让海岸上沙子的颜色变得更浅，跟溢出的锈色污水混在了一起。

En: Look around you.
Zh: 看看自己周围。

En: How does it feel?
Zh: 感觉怎么样？

En: What is there?
Zh: 那里有什么？

En: Below the old mines -- L'Ossuaire Municipal, Revachol's underground cemetery. *Les petits rats* brave the underground passageways, trying to get to Le Royaume...
Zh: 在旧矿井下面——是市公墓，瑞瓦肖的地下墓地。那群*小老鼠*勇敢地面对着地下通道，想要抵达王国……

En: Look around.
Zh: 环顾四周。

En: A chill comes over you, crawling down your back. The sand under your feet is wet. Somewhere in the south, tarpaulin flap in the wind.
Zh: 你感到一阵寒意，顺着你的后背窜了下去。脚下是潮湿的沙子。南边的某个地方，防水油布在风中翻飞。

En: For what?
Zh: 为什么？

En: Someone's stuffed a big old polar anorak into a concrete pipe under the boardwalk. It would keep you warm. You will probably never happen across it, but who knows.
Zh: 有人把一件又大又旧的厚夹克塞进了木板道下的一根水泥管里。它能帮你保暖。你可能永远也不会遇到它，但是谁知道呢。

En: Tell me a secret of the sands, wind.
Zh: 风，给我讲一个沙子的秘密吧。

En: No need for secrets.
Zh: 不需要什么秘密。

En: No need for anything anymore.
Zh: 什么也不需要了。

En: But still...
Zh: 但还是……

En: Where is Ruby?
Zh: 露比在哪？

En: Near. Snow falls into dark water. She prays, though she doesn't believe. There is a hum in the air.
Zh: 附近。雪花飘落进黑水里。她祈祷着，虽然她并不相信。空气中传来嗡嗡的声音。

En: That's all.
Zh: 就这些。

En: That's where Cuno said he's gonna go...
Zh: 那就是坤诺说他要去的地方……

En: Yes. To peel the gold off with his fingernails.
Zh: 是的。用他的手指甲把黄金抠下来。

En: The spring thaw will come in earnest. Just not yet.
Zh: 春季解冻期会郑重其事地到来。只是还没到而已。

En: Stomp your feet for warmth, brushing off the snow. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 跺跺脚取暖，拍掉雪花。[完成思维。]

En: We should keep moving. Who knows when this snow will let up?
Zh: “我们应该继续前进。谁知道这场雪什么时候会变小？”

En: What's in the east?
Zh: 东边有什么？

En: What's in the west?
Zh: 西边有什么？

En: Shake the shivers off. [Discard thought.]
Zh: 甩掉这种颤抖的感觉。[放弃思维]

En: What's in the south?
Zh: 南边有什么？

En: Winter, slow to let go of Revachol, flecks some more wet snow from above...
Zh: 冬季，慢慢放开了瑞瓦肖，从天空洒下更多湿漉漉的雪花……

En: What am I doing?
Zh: 我在做什么？

En: And to think -- it seemed as though it were already spring.
Zh: 想一想——似乎春天已经到来。

En: What's in the north?
Zh: 北边有什么？

En: It has been a cold year. All things waiting for spring will have to wait a bit longer.
Zh: 今年特别冷。世间万物都在等待春天的到来，只不过可能还要等得再久一点。

En: You should be warm, with your hat and your coat, but you notice that you're missing a shoe. You feel like a traveller ill-equipped to survey the landscape you have set out to explore.
Zh: 有了帽子和大衣，你应该感到十分温暖，但你注意到自己少了一只鞋。你感觉就像个装备不良的旅行者，正探索着这片大地。

En: You should be warm, with your hat and your coat, but you notice that you don't have any shoes on. You feel like a traveller ill-equipped to survey the landscape you have endeavoured to explore.
Zh: 有了帽子和大衣，你应该感到十分温暖，但你注意自己没穿鞋。你感觉自己是个装备不良的旅行者，正探索着这片大地。

En: You're warm in your hat and your coat. Like a well-equipped explorer, you comfortably survey the white landscape.
Zh: 穿戴着帽子和大衣的你感到十分温暖。你就像个装备精良的探险家，舒适地探查着这片白茫茫的大地。

En: Looking up at the sky, melted snow dripping from your hat.
Zh: 抬头望向天空，融化的雪花从你的帽子上滴落下来。

En: Looking up at the sky, melted snow dripping from your hair...
Zh: 抬头望向天空，融化的雪花从你的头发上滴落下来……

En: What do I see?
Zh: 我看见什么了？

En: The sky is full of great battleships. White clouds collide with one another, and the world is drenched with their debris.
Zh: 天空满是大型战舰。白色的云朵相互碰撞着，整个世界都被它们的残骸所遮盖。

En: How does it feel?
Zh: 感觉怎么样？

En: The melting snow seeps through your thin clothes. The toes of your one bare foot are growing numb. At least your hair is protected from getting wet and sticking together. You look around...
Zh: 融化的雪花渗入你单薄的衣衫。那只光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。至少你的头发有了保护，不会湿乎乎地黏在一起。你看了看周围……

En: The melting snow seeps through your thin clothes. The toes of your bare feet are growing numb. At least your hair is protected from getting wet and sticking together. You look around...
Zh: 融化的雪花渗入你单薄的衣衫。你光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。至少你的头发有了保护，不会湿乎乎地黏在一起。你看了看周围……

En: The melting snow seeps through your thin clothes. Grandmothers don't let their grandchildren out dressed so lightly in this weather. At least your hair is protected from getting wet and sticking together. You look around...
Zh: 融化的雪花渗入你单薄的衣衫。在这种天气里，祖母们不会让他们的外孙穿的如此轻薄。至少你的头发有了保护，不会湿乎乎地黏在一起。你看了看周围……

En: Your teeth chatter as the snow melts on your exposed skin, running down your chest and your back in icy rivulets. The toes of your one bare foot are growing numb. Your hat is of little help. To distract yourself, you look around...
Zh: 雪花融化在你裸露的皮肤上，化成一条冰冷的小溪沿着你的胸膛和后背缓缓滑落，让你的牙齿止不住的打战。那只光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。帽子没能帮上你多大的忙。为了转移注意力，你看向周围……

En: Your teeth chatter as the snow melts on your exposed skin, running down your chest and your back in icy rivulets. Your bare feet are growing numb. Your hat is of little help. To distract yourself, you look around...
Zh: 雪花融化在你裸露的皮肤上，化成一条冰冷的小溪沿着你的胸膛和后背缓缓滑落，让你的牙齿止不住的打战。光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。帽子没能帮上你多大的忙。为了转移注意力，你看向周围……

En: Your teeth chatter as the snow melts on your exposed skin, running down your chest and your back in icy rivulets. Your hat is of little help. To distract yourself, you look around...
Zh: 雪花融化在你裸露的皮肤上，化成一条冰冷的小溪沿着你的胸膛和后背缓缓滑落，让你的牙齿止不住的打战。帽子没能帮上你多大的忙。为了转移注意力，你看向周围……

En: You're warm in your coat, though your cold, damp hair sticks to your head unpleasantly, and your one bare foot is growing numb. You survey the landscape around you.
Zh: 裹在大衣里的你感觉很温暖，冰冷潮湿的头发贴在头皮上，让人感觉有些不悦，而且那只光脚变得越来越麻木。你观察着四周的环境。

En: You're warm in your coat, though your cold, damp hair sticks to your head unpleasantly, and your bare feet are growing numb. You survey the landscape around you.
Zh: 裹在大衣里的你感觉很温暖，冰冷潮湿的头发贴在头皮上，让人感觉有些不悦，而且光着的脚变得越来越麻木。你观察着四周的环境。

En: You're warm in your coat, though your cold, damp hair sticks to your head unpleasantly -- you should have a hat. You survey the landscape around you.
Zh: 裹在大衣里的你感觉很温暖，冰冷潮湿的头发贴在头皮上，让人感觉有些不悦——你应该弄一顶帽子。你观察着四周的环境。

En: The melting snow seeps through your thin clothes. The toes of your one bare foot are growing numb. You look around...
Zh: 融化的雪花渗入你单薄的衣衫。那只光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。你看了看周围……

En: The melting snow seeps through your thin clothes. The toes of your bare feet are growing numb. You look around...
Zh: 融化的雪花渗入你单薄的衣衫。光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。你看了看周围……

En: The melting snow seeps through your thin clothes. Grandmothers don't let their grandchildren out dressed so lightly in this weather. You look around...
Zh: 融化的雪花渗入你单薄的衣衫。在这种天气里，祖母们不会让他们的外孙穿的如此轻薄。你看了看周围……

En: Your teeth chatter as the snow melts on your exposed skin, running down your chest and your back in icy rivulets. The toes of your one bare foot are growing numb. To distract yourself, you look around...
Zh: 雪花融化在你裸露的皮肤上，化成一条冰冷的小溪沿着你的胸膛和后背缓缓滑落，让你的牙齿止不住的打战。那只光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。为了转移注意力，你看向周围……

En: Your teeth chatter as the snow melts on your exposed skin, running down your chest and your back in icy rivulets. Your bare feet are growing numb. To distract yourself, you look around...
Zh: 雪花融化在你裸露的皮肤上，化成一条冰冷的小溪沿着你的胸膛和后背缓缓滑落，让你的牙齿止不住的打战。光脚的脚趾变得越来越麻木。为了转移注意力，你看向周围……

En: Your teeth chatter as the snow melts on your exposed skin, running down your chest and your back in icy rivulets. To distract yourself, you look around...
Zh: 雪花融化在你裸露的皮肤上，化成一条冰冷的小溪沿着你的胸膛和后背缓缓滑落，让你的牙齿止不住的打战。为了转移注意力，你看向周围……

En: The lieutenant looks at you standing there shivering with some concern, but makes no comment.
Zh: 警督略带担忧地看着站在那儿浑身颤抖的你，但是没有发表任何评论。

En: The lieutenant looks at your feet with some concern, but makes no comment.
Zh: 警督略呆担忧地看着你的脚，但是没有发表任何评论。

En: The canal you crossed to get here, and beyond it -- Martinaise proper, the district the police forgot to police. There is laughter, lights, attempts at entrepreneurial activity, cynicism.
Zh: 在你之前穿越的那条运河上面——是马丁内斯，被警察遗忘的区域。那里有欢笑、灯光、创业活动尝试、还有犬儒主义。

En: Someone is scraping snow off their windshield. At the roundabout, in the midst of which a statue of Filippe the III serves as a destination for grade-school field trips and a fine perch for winter birds.
Zh: 有人刮着挡风玻璃上的雪。环岛中央伫立着一座菲利普三世的雕像，这里是小学户外旅行的目的地，也是冬候鸟良好的栖息地。

En: That's all?
Zh: 就这些？

En: Like all monuments, it's also a warning. Six-story ruins on the south side of the roundabout cast a long shadow over the profligate king.
Zh: 就像所有纪念碑一样，它也是个警告。环岛南面六层高的废墟在这位挥霍无度的国王身上投下了长长的阴影。

En: More winding coastline lined with abandoned buildings. Crumbling piers, salt water lapping at their dark piles. Grey and red, forgotten city blocks. What remains of the pre-revolutionary effort to gentrify the coast.
Zh: 蜿蜒的海岸线边排列着更多的废弃建筑。破损的码头上，咸咸的海水拍打着黑色桩柱。还有那灰红色的、被人遗忘的城市街区。大革命之前修复海岸做出的努力，如今就只剩下如此光景。

En: And beyond that?
Zh: 除此之外呢？

En: The waters turn black. Coal City in the shadow of Saint-Martin, a boom town, back when coal extracted from countless shafts near the city was needed to power Revachol.
Zh: 海水变黑了。煤城笼罩在圣马丁的阴影之下，那是一座新兴城市，以前煤炭会从城市周围数不清的竖井里开采出来，为瑞瓦肖提供能源。

En: No more. The coal was supplanted by petroleum from the ocean floor and hydropower from the Esperance. Everything crumbled. These days, only the weakest remain in Coal City. Their hopes of getting rich linger in the defunct shafts under their feet.
Zh: 现在没有了。煤炭被海床里的石油和埃斯佩兰斯河的水力所替代。一切都崩溃了。现如今，只有最虚弱的人才会留在煤城。他们致富的希望还在脚下废弃的竖井里苟延残喘。

En: Layer upon layer of sand poisoned with industrial run-off. The storm drainage. Hidden bunkers. Rats scuttle...
Zh: 一层又一层被工业废水污染的沙子。雨水管道。隐蔽的碉堡。老鼠四下逃窜……

En: What's below me?
Zh: 我下面是什么？

En: What's above?
Zh: 上面是什么？

En: Coalition aerostatics hang like apparitions under the cloud cover. Occasionally, lights flash as they manoeuvre through the falling snow.
Zh: 联盟的静风舰像幽灵般漂浮在云层下面。它们在飘雪中穿过的时候，偶尔会留下几分闪烁的灯光。

En: More coalition aerostatics. Occasionally, lights flash as they manoeuvre through the falling snow. One large airship carries crates in its belly.
Zh: 更多联盟的静风舰。它们在飘雪中穿过的时候，偶尔会留下几分闪烁的灯光。一艘大飞艇下面运载着板条箱。

En: Le Royaume...
Zh: 王国……

En: Les *petits rats*...
Zh: *小老鼠*……

En: Deeper...
Zh: 再深一些……

En: ...where the Filippian kings were interred, with their doctors and their admirals. Mausoleums, burial chambers, leaf gold still remains on the Double Door of the Morning.
Zh: ……是菲利普家族的国王们，他们的医生，还有舰队司令被埋葬的地方。陵墓，墓室，金叶还留在双扇门上。

En: Children under 14. They go underground, looking for artefacts to sell to foreign museums -- and for fabled relics. Their parents let them. They go deeper...
Zh: 14岁以下的孩子。他们深入地下，寻找可以卖给外国博物馆的文物——还有传说中的圣物。这是他们父母的授意。他们越来越深入……

En: ...after rubies, melchiorite, lapis lazuli plundered from Safre and Seol during the time of the Suzerain. In the burial chambers of the kings: Grand Old Filippe, Guillaume II, and even in the mausoleum of Filippe the Opulent.
Zh: ……在宗主国时期，从萨弗里和西奥掠夺红宝石、白铜石和青金石之后。在国王的墓室里：老菲利普国王，纪尧姆二世，甚至还有丰饶者菲利普的陵墓。

En: Wipe the snow from your shoulder.
Zh: 拍掉肩膀上的雪。

En: Two kilometres underground, in a winding shaft along whose walls mirrors have been placed so that daylight may eternally fall upon the richest of all the kings.
Zh: 地下两千米深处，一个提升井的墙壁上挂着很多面镜子，这样阳光就能永远地照射在最富有的国王身上。

En: The mausoleum contains untold quantities of gold -- and that special, purest-of-the-pure magenta cocaine favoured by Revacholian royalty.
Zh: 陵墓里蕴藏着数不清的黄金——还有瑞瓦肖前皇室钟爱的那种最特别、最纯净的洋红色可卡因。

En: How can it be pure if it's magenta?
Zh: 如果是洋红色的话，还怎么能算是最纯净的呢？

En: Did someone say *untold quantities of cocaine*? Drop everything immediately and go looking for this hoard!
Zh: 刚是不是有人说了*数不清的可卡因*？赶快放下手中的一切，去寻找这个宝藏吧！

En: How can it be pure if it's magenta?
Zh: 如果是洋红色的话，还怎么能算是最纯净的呢？

En: Few *petits rats* return from the shafts -- and even fewer find what they're looking for. A small child steps out of a black tunnel, with silver trinkets in her pockets.
Zh: 只有少数*小老鼠*从竖井里回来了——甚至更少数找到了他们要找的东西。一个小孩从黑色的隧道中钻了出来，口袋里还装着一些银色的小饰品。

En: All around her, white snow on the extinguished coke furnaces, and on the weather-worn shacks, where fathers beat their sons after drinking. The snow melts on your fingers, turning to water.
Zh: 在她周围，白雪覆盖在熄灭的焦炭炉上。在被风雨侵蚀的棚屋里，醉酒的父亲正在殴打他们的儿子。雪花在你的指尖融化成水滴。

En: Snow falls and there is no answer. Some things are not for mortals to know.
Zh: 雪花飘落，无人应答。有些事情，凡人无法知晓。

En: And further...
Zh: 更远处……

En: A fenced-off yard. There's a truck belonging to a logistics company parked next to the gate. You've seen it. Bright light from a building behind the fence reflects off its hood.
Zh: 一个围起来的院子。大门旁边停靠着一辆物流公司的货車。你之前见过。围栏后建筑发出的亮光反射在货車的引擎盖上。

En: A fenced-off yard. There's a truck belonging to a logistics company parked next to the gate. You've heard about it. Bright light from a building behind the fence reflects off its hood.
Zh: 一个围起来的院子。大门旁边停靠着一辆物流公司的货車。你之前听说过。围栏后建筑发出的亮光反射在货車的引擎盖上。

En: A fenced-off yard. There's a truck belonging to a logistics company parked next to the gate. Bright light from a building behind the fence reflects off its hood.
Zh: 一个被栅栏围起来的院子。一台德尔塔物流公司的货車停靠在大门旁边。栅栏背后，一栋建筑透出的明亮灯光映照在它的引擎盖上。

En: Clean white light, coming from the windows of a clean cube-shaped office building hidden amidst ruins. A secret...
Zh: 干净的白光从窗户中投射出来，那是一栋干净的方形办公大楼，就隐藏在废墟中央。一个秘密……

En: The raised motorway, 8/81, with vehicles whooshing past one another day and night, while those who reside beneath the motorway attempt to carry on with their lives in the snow and the slush. And south of the 8/81 is the Pox.
Zh: 高架高速公路，8/81，形形色色的車辆日日夜夜从上面呼啸而过，而那些居住在高速公路下面的人，努力在大雪和烂泥中维系他们的生活。8/81的南边是帕克斯。

En: ...was once a park, a place for reflection and recuperation for the patients of the Old Military Hospital. In the Twenties, it was used as a quarantine centre during a measles outbreak that killed many children. Most everyone has avoided the hospital and surrounding park ever since.
Zh: ……那里曾经是一个公园，旧军事医院的病人用来静神养心的地方。在20年代那场让很多孩童丧命的麻疹爆发期间，这里被当成了一个检疫中心。从那之后，几乎所有人都避开了医院和邻近的公园。

En: The Pox is completely wild now. Evergreen thickets covered in snow and industrial dust. Feral dogs and even wolves roaming in packs. The police try to keep the deepest corners cordoned off.
Zh: 现在的帕克斯已经是一片荒野。常绿灌木丛被掩藏在大雪和工业粉尘之下。成群的野狗，甚至还有野狼在这里游荡。警察试图封锁最深处的那些角落。

En: ...heavy drug users do slip through and hole up in the Old Military Hospital, hoping to find something to get high on among the hastily abandoned supplies. Or just to overdose in peace.
Zh: ……重度吸毒者溜进来，偷偷藏在旧军事医院里，希望能在匆忙丢弃的补给品中找到一些能让自己兴奋起来的东西。或者只想平静地享受服药过量的感觉。

En: Further south...
Zh: 南边更远处……

En: A line of motor garages with armoured carapaces, hunched in the cold. A mechanic is hard at work, patching up bullet holes in the side of a Coupris 40. These are the garages of Precinct 41. Snow settles on the roof of the re-purposed silk mill that serves as your station. Shivering RCM personnel hurry in and out of the main entrance.
Zh: 一排装甲車库在寒冷中蜷缩着。一名机械师埋头工作，修补着库普瑞斯侧面的弹孔。这些是41分局的車库。雪花飘落在丝绸厂的屋顶，这里已经被重新翻修成你的警局。RCM的工作人员哆哆嗦嗦地从大门入口进出。

En: Patrol Officer Judit Minot stands in the corner, beneath the narrow awning that hangs over the main entrance. She is watching the snow absent-mindedly as she searches for her gloves in her briefcase.
Zh: 巡警茱蒂特·迈诺特站在角落——悬挂在大门上方狭窄雨篷的下面。她心不在焉地看着漫天的雪花，在公文包里翻找着自己的手套。

En: Where are hell are they... she mutters under her breath.
Zh: “到底去哪了……”她小声嘀咕着。

En: It's not like that. They're what is called *hetero-sexual life partners*. They have a battle-tested relationship. A 'bröderbund', if you will.
Zh: “不是这样的。他们是大家口中的*异性恋生活伴侣*。彼此之间的关系久经考验。如果你喜欢的话，可以称之为‘兄弟情'。”

En: No, nothing. It's just... Judit went to his place and found the Monday mail unopened. I think he's still there. You didn't, like, drink with him over the weekend did you?
Zh: “没，没什么。只不过……茱蒂特去他那看了看，发现周一的邮件没开过封。我觉得他应该还在*那里*。你不会……周末跟他一起喝酒去了吧？”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: With that animal? Never again, man. What, is he still down there, in... you know, south of the interchange? The... what was it?
Zh: “跟那只野兽吗？再也不会了，伙计。什么，你说他还在那里，在……你知道的，枢纽站南边？那个……是什么来着？”

En: Huh? Yeah. He takes out his gloves and struggles to pull them onto his hands.
Zh: “哈？是啊。”他掏出手套，挣扎着戴在手上。

En: Satellite-Officer Jean Vicquemare rushes down the station stairs, angrily hunched over in the falling slush.
Zh: 随迁警官让·维克玛冲下警局的楼梯，怒气冲冲地踩在泥浆里。

En: Hetero... sexual. Life... partners.
Zh: “异性……恋。生活……伴侣。”

En: That would have been irresponsible.
Zh: “那也太不负责任了吧。”

En: Funny apery. Vicquemare finally manages to pull his gloves on. There's a small tear along the inside seam of the left glove. "Male-centric workplace humour. Have you seen him?"
Zh: “你这模仿的有点意思啊。”维克玛总算戴上了手套。左边手套的内侧缝线处有些小开裂。“男权中心工作场所的幽默。你见着他了吗？”

En: In Martinaise. He's in Martinaise.
Zh: “在马丁内斯。他在马丁内斯。”

En: Is there something wrong?
Zh: “那里出什么事了吗？”

En: Where's your homo, homey?
Zh: “你的同性恋朋友去哪了，基佬？”

En: On the bridge, Officers Torson and McLaine are standing guard. The muscular Torson wears jeans and a leather jacket. When Vicquemare passes him, the young man remarks...
Zh: 托森和麦克莱恩警官在桥上守卫。强壮的托森穿着牛仔裤和一件皮夹克。维克玛走过的时候，那个年轻人评论到……

En: Around you, the snow continues to fall. To the west, the ocean swells.
Zh: 雪在你周围不断下落。西面的大海在涌浪。

En: It's the only home you have now. But you can't go back. Not like this.
Zh: 现在这是你唯一的家了。但你不能回去，像这样不行。

En: The raised motorway, 8/81, separating Martinaise from Jamrock. Vehicles whoosh past one another day and night, while those who reside in the labyrinthine alleyways beneath the motorway attempt to carry on with their lives in the snow and the slush. And south of the 8/81 is the Pox.
Zh: 高架高速公路8/81，把马丁内斯和加姆洛克分隔开来。車辆夜以继日地从上面呼啸而过，而那些住在桥下复杂小巷里的人，还在大雪和泥泞里努力维系自己的生活。8/81南边是帕克斯。

En: The Pox...
Zh: 帕克斯……

En: No, it was home. I want more.
Zh: 不，这是家。我想要更多。

En: The stairs descend -- to Central Jamrock. A man named Kuklov has a snow-covered stall there, in the market across the bridge. He sells kebab infested with fly larvae to your colleagues who believe eating it will make them immune to food poisoning.
Zh: 楼梯向下——延伸至加姆洛克中心。一个名叫库科洛夫的男人在那里摆了个被白雪覆盖的小摊，就在桥对面的市场里。他把感染了苍蝇幼虫的烤肉串卖给你的同事们，他们相信吃掉之后就能免疫食物中毒。

En: The surgeon watches her for a moment, pulling on his cigarette. "You know, it's not so cold that you're going to get frostbite without your gloves."
Zh: 外科医生看了她一会儿，然后抽了一口烟。“你知道吗，天气还没冷到不戴手套就会冻伤的地步。”

En: She nods, then looks down at her hands. No frostbite, sure, but a web of thin red cracks around the wrists. She is visibly embarrassed by the delicacy of her skin.
Zh: 她点点头，然后低头看着自己的手。当然，没有冻伤，但是手腕周围有一圈细细的红色裂纹。她显然对自己娇嫩的皮肤感到有些尴尬。

En: Suddenly, she smiles at some thought that had just come to her, nods to the surgeon again, and sprints down the stairs, gloveless, into the falling snow.
Zh: 突然，她的脑海里冒出一些让自己发笑的想法。于是，她朝外科医生又点了点头，快步冲下楼梯，站在雪中——手上没戴手套。

En: Snow falls on the utility district: the library, the florist, the Saramirizian restaurant that offers homemade wine. And also on the brothels and drug dens, and the chop shops of the zemlyaki.
Zh: 雪花飘落在这个公用事业区——图书馆，花店，还有贩卖自家美酒的萨拉米尔泽餐厅。还有妓院，毒贩老巢和泽姆拉基的拆車厂。

En: All of this built around a lake that formed in a meteorite strike. At the centre of this lake, there is a little ship. There are lights at the bottom of its hull. They are lights directed toward the sea floor, looking for something, like whiskers...
Zh: 那是一个陨石撞击形成的湖泊，所有建筑都环湖而建。在湖心中央，有一只小船。船体底部有一些灯。它们直直地照射着海床，寻找着什么东西，仿佛晶须一般……

En: Wonder if Vic's found his hetero-sexual life partner yet. The man in the fishnet wifebeater looks over at Chester McLaine.
Zh: “不知道维克有没有找到他的异性恋生活伴侣。”穿着渔网背心的男人看向切斯特·麦克莱恩。

En: Damn, I don't know. Even a real *bröderbund* like that can't survive everything...
Zh: “见鬼，我不知道。就算是真正的*兄弟情*也不会永久幸存下来吧……”

En: The abandoned church. One of two remaining stave churches which were collectively called les Sept Soeurs. The other six sisters were destroyed during the Revolution.
Zh: 废弃的教堂。仅存的两座木条教堂的其中之一，它们被统称为“七姐妹”。另外五个姐妹都在大革命期间毁掉了。

En: The abandoned church, its walls covered with hoarfrost now. One of les Sept Soeurs. The other six sisters were destroyed during the Revolution.
Zh: 废弃的教堂，现在墙壁上布满白霜。它是七姐妹之一。另外五个姐妹都在大革命期间毁掉了。

En: An abandoned church that's being turned into a dance club. Four youths are hard at work there, making it ever harder core.
Zh: 一座废弃的教堂，如今变成了一家夜店。四个年轻人在那里埋头苦干，让它变得更加硬核了。

En: And further north?
Zh: 再往北呢？

En: A serpentine strip of land weaving its way into the Martinaise inlet. Unfortunates on the run -- from the law, from themselves -- sometimes hide out on nearby islets. Little dots in the ocean that are occasionally submerged when the tide is high and the weather foul.
Zh: 一块蜿蜒狭长的土地，一路延伸进马丁内斯水湾。那些逃亡的人——逃避法律制裁，逃避自己的可怜虫——有时会隐藏在附近的小岛上。那些海洋中的小斑点偶尔会在涨潮和坏天气的时候被水淹没。

En: Then there's Ozonne... but the snow falls too thick. You cannot see that far.
Zh: 然后就是欧佐纳尔……但是雪堆积地太厚。你看不到那么远。

En: The remains of a camp on a jagged piece of rock -- a tent, old dishes and cutlery. Long since abandoned. A hermit crab scuttles among the debris, looking for a new shell.
Zh: 一块锯齿状的岩石上残留着一片营地遗迹——只有一顶帐篷，一堆老旧的碗碟和餐具。这里已经废弃很久了。一只寄居蟹在废墟中乱窜，寻找着新的躯壳。

En: Further out, the lights burn bright on Résurrection; way beyond Martinaise -- a popular spa destination for ample-bodied Ozonne kids with equally ample pockets.
Zh: 较远的地方，又是一片灯火通明；离马丁内斯很远的地方——是一个热门的休闲渡假村，那里满是身材圆润的欧佐纳尔小孩，还有他们同样圆圆鼓鼓的钱包。

En: Before that? Before the curtains are drawn...
Zh: 在那之前呢？在帷幔落下之前……

En: The Bay of Revachol, vastness, great depth -- over 1200 m at its deepest. Water, air brinier than here. It is crisscrossed by huge cargo ships bearing company logos: Wild Pines, ZAMM, Moriyn.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖海湾，旷阔无垠，深不可测——最深的地方超过1200米。水和空气都比这里要咸很多。印着公司标志的巨型货船在水面上来来往往：野松公司，ZAMM，莫里林……

En: And, at the farthest reaches of the Bay of Revachol -- the shadow of Coalition Warship Archer, on perpetual patrol duty, ready to unleash artillery fire if you were to rise up against the market. You shudder.
Zh: 在瑞瓦肖海湾最遥远的流域——是联盟军舰“弓箭手”的阴影，正在执行不间断地巡逻任务，如果你想奋起反抗市场，就要随时做好迎接炮火洗礼的准备。你颤抖了。

En: And on the islets?
Zh: 那小岛上有什么？

En: And on the other side of the inlet?
Zh: 那水湾另一边有什么？

En: I like regular, normal things.
Zh: 我喜欢普通又寻常的东西。

En: Oops, it's a fashion faux pas! [Discard the thought -- and the pants.]
Zh: 哎呀，对时尚还真是太失礼了！[放弃思维——还有这条裤子。]

En: Let's hope no one saw you wearing those outrageously regular -- almost moralist! -- pants.
Zh: 希望没人看见你穿着这条普通地相当凶残——几乎可以说像个道德家！——的裤子。

En: Makes sense. This is what wearing boring office trousers does to you.
Zh: 有道理。穿上无聊的工装裤就会对你造成这种影响。

En: You're a little more moralist now, buddy. A little more *normal*. Even if you didn't want to be.
Zh: 现在你变得更像个道德家了，伙计。变得更*正常*一些了。就算这不是你想要的结果。

En: Mhm, I know you do. These interisolary pants are like wearing a perfect *compromise* in your nether regions. No one will call the Moralintern on you like this, that's for sure.
Zh: 嗯，我知道你会的。这些跨洲裤就像是给你的股间穿上了一件完美的*妥协*。这样没人会叫国际道德伦理委员会来抓你，这一点是肯定的。

En: Mm, these are some wonderfully regular pants. Not too tight, not too loose -- moderate in every sense. You'll blend right in at some pleasant dinner party.
Zh: 嗯，这些是非常普通的裤子。不太紧，也不太松——从各种意义上讲都很适中。你可以很快融入愉快的晚宴中。
